| Dot product forward and running-error core | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/DotProduct.lean` | `fl_dotProduct`, `dotProductLocalFactor`, `runningErrorMu`, `fl_runningDotProduct` | `dotProduct_factor_expansion_succ`, `dotProduct_factor_expansion_sum_succ`, `dotProductLocalFactor_abs_sub_one_le_101`, `dotProduct_error_bound_101_succ`, `dotProduct_error_bound`, `exactDotProductPrefixState_last_eq_sum`, `runningError_bound_from_local_errors`, `fl_runningDotProduct_error_bound_from_inverse_models` | Source-shaped local-factor expansion for positive-length sequential dot products, the Higham p.75 `1.01*n*u` small-`nu` forward bound under `0 < fp.u` and `(n : Real)*fp.u < 0.01`, the tight `gamma fp n` forward bound, and the concrete Algorithm 3.2 running-error loop. The running-error theorem builds stored rounded products and partial sums and proves `|fl_runningDotProduct - x^T y| <= u*runningErrorMu` from operation-level inverse-model witnesses for each actual `fl_mul` and `fl_add`; deriving those witnesses from the primitive standard `FPModel` remains a separate model-foundation obligation. |
| Complex dot and matrix-vector backward error | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/ComplexBackwardError.lean` | `complexDotProduct`, `complexDotProductRelErrorExpansion`, `complexMatVec`, `complexMatVecRelErrorRows` | `complexDotProduct_backward_stable_y`, `complexDotProduct_backward_stable_x`, `complexMatVec_backward_error` | Closes Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.7 at the algebraic transfer level promised by §3.6. A complex dot-product expansion `computed=sum_i x_i y_i(1+eta_i)` with `||eta_i||<=gamma` gives either `computed=sum_i x_i(y_i+Delta y_i)` with `||Delta y_i||<=gamma||y_i||`, or `computed=sum_i (x_i+Delta x_i)y_i` with `||Delta x_i||<=gamma||x_i||`. Row-wise expansions give the complex matrix-vector analogue of (3.10): `computed=(A+DeltaA)x` with `||DeltaA_ij||<=gamma||A_ij||`. The constants are intentionally supplied as the complex-operation budget from Lemma 3.5 or its downstream dot-product analysis. |
| Continued-fraction running error | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/ContinuedFraction.lean` | `continuedFractionStep`, `continuedFractionStepAmplification` | `continuedFraction_step_error_le`, `continuedFraction_running_error_bound` | Closes Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.3 at the recurrence-error level. The one-step theorem proves that a local residual `tau` and inherited denominator error `muNext` give `|qhat-q| <= tau + |b|*muNext/(|qhatNext|*(|qhatNext|-muNext))`, provided `muNext < |qhatNext|`. The running-error theorem packages these step inequalities into a reverse-induction certificate for the full backward recurrence, including the terminal bound. |

| Block dot products | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/BlockDotProduct.lean` | `fl_blockDotProduct` | `blockDotProduct_backward_error`, `blockDotProduct_error_bound`, `twoPieceDotProduct_error_bound`, `blockDotProduct_real_index_decomposition`, `blockDotProduct_real_index_ge_optimum`, `blockDotProduct_real_index_at_sqrt` | Equal-block Chapter 3 p.70 route. For `q+1` blocks of length `b`, the algorithm has radius `gamma fp (b+q)`, i.e. `gamma_{n/k+k-1}` when `n=b*k` and `k=q+1`; the two-piece theorem gives `gamma fp (b+1)`. The real-index lemmas formalize the continuous balancing rule `k ~= sqrt(n)`. |

The standard inner route gives the displayed `u*|x^T y| + (1+u)*gamma_e(n)*|x|^T|y|` bound; the exact-product route reduces the inner gamma index to `n-1`. |

| Matrix-matrix normwise forward, inverse backward, and componentwise backward-error lower bounds | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/MatMul.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/MatMulBackwardError.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | `fl_matMul`, `oneNorm`, `oneNormRect`, `infNormRect`, `frobNorm`, `frobNormRect`, `rectMatMul`, `rectOpNorm2Le`, `IsInverse`, `matMulResidual`, `matMulRelativeMajorant`, `matMulRelativeBackwardFeasible`, `matMulMixedBackwardForwardFeasible` | `matMul_error_bound_frobNorm_majorant`, `matMul_error_bound_rectOpNorm2Le_majorant`, `matMul_error_bound_oneNorm`, `matMul_error_bound_oneNormRect`, `matMul_error_bound_infNormRect`, `matMul_error_bound_frobNormRect`, `matMul_error_bound_frobNorm`, `matMul_error_bound_opNorm2Le_frob`, `matMul_backward_error_common_A_of_inverse`, `matMul_backward_error_common_B_of_inverse`, `matMulRelativeBackwardFeasible_residual_entry_le`, `matMulRelativeBackwardFeasible_sqrt_lower_bound_entry` | Norm-level Chapter 3 corollaries from (3.12): the rectangular majorant `||fl(AB)-AB||_F <= gamma_n || |A||B| ||_F`, square and rectangular maximum-column-sum product bounds, a rectangular maximum-row-sum product bound, square and rectangular Frobenius product bounds `||fl(AB)-AB||_F <= gamma_n ||A||_F ||B||_F`, and a predicate-form `p = 2` vector-action majorant: `rectOpNorm2Le |A| alpha` and `rectOpNorm2Le |B| beta` imply a `rectOpNorm2Le` certificate for the error at radius `gamma_n*alpha*beta`. Problem 3.5 is closed for square nonsingular factors: `matMul_backward_error_common_A_of_inverse` proves `fl(A*B) = (A+DeltaA)B` with `|DeltaA| <= gamma_n |A||B||B_inv|`, and `matMul_backward_error_common_B_of_inverse` proves `fl(A*B) = A(B+DeltaB)` with `|DeltaB| <= gamma_n |A_inv||A||B|`. Problem 3.6 is closed for the relative componentwise majorant `G=|A||B|`: every feasible radius satisfies `|R_ij| <= ((1+epsilon)^2-1)G_ij`, hence `sqrt(1+|R_ij|/G_ij)-1 <= epsilon` when `G_ij>0`. The mixed backward/forward feasibility predicate is exposed for the rank-deficient/general case. The Frobenius-radius operator certificate remains as a coarser convenience; the repository does not introduce a supremum-valued spectral norm function for legacy function-shaped matrices. |

Factor bounds `N(Delta X_j) <= delta_j N(X_j)` with `0 <= delta_j` imply `N(prod_j (X_j+Delta X_j)-prod_j X_j) <= (prod_j(1+delta_j)-1) prod_j N(X_j)`. |
| Matrix-product componentwise perturbation | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | `matSeqProd`, `scalarSeqProd`, `absMatrix` | `scalarSeqProd_nonneg`, `one_le_scalarSeqProd`, `matSeqProd_nonneg`, `matSeqProd_abs_perturbed_le_scalar_abs`, `matSeqProd_componentwise_perturbation_bound` | Closes Higham Chapter 3 Lemma 3.7 for finite left-to-right products of square real matrices: componentwise factor bounds `|Delta X_j| <= delta_j |X_j|` with `0 <= delta_j` imply `|prod_j (X_j+Delta X_j)-prod_j X_j| <= (prod_j (1+delta_j)-1) prod_j |X_j|` componentwise. |
| Sequential matrix-product accumulation | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/MatSeqProduct.lean` | `scalarPrefixProd`, `matPrefixProd`, `matPrefixProdError` | `scalarPrefixProd_nonneg`, `matPrefixProd_error_bound_from_local_errors`, `matPrefixProd_error_bound_uniform` | Closes the finite accumulation core of Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.8. If `Phat_{j+1}=Phat_j A_j+E_j`, each right factor has a predicate-style operator-2 certificate `alpha_j`, and `||E_j||_F <= eps_j alpha_0...alpha_j`, then `||Phat_k-A_0...A_{k-1}||_F <= (sum_{j<k} eps_j) alpha_0...alpha_{k-1}`. The uniform corollary gives `k*beta*alpha_0...alpha_{k-1}`, the concrete finite-budget form behind the source's `(k n^2 u + O(u^2)) ||A_1||_2...||A_k||_2` display once the local matrix-multiply budget is instantiated. |
| Difference of squares | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/SquareDifference.lean` | `fl_squareDiff_direct`, `fl_squareDiff_factored` | `squareDiff_factor_identity`, `fl_squareDiff_direct_error_bound`, `fl_squareDiff_factored_rel_error`, `fl_squareDiff_factored_error_bound` | Closes Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.9 at the standard-model error-bound level. The direct route `fl(fl(x*x)-fl(y*y))` is bounded by `gamma_2*(|x*x|+|y*y|)`, while the factored route `fl(fl(x+y)*fl(x-y))` satisfies a relative-error form with `|theta| <= gamma_3` and hence an absolute bound `gamma_3*|x^2-y^2|`. This formalizes why the factored route is preferable when the final difference is small. |
| Wilkinson powers-of-two summation family | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/WilkinsonAttainability.lean` | `wilkinsonProblem42BlockValue`, `wilkinsonProblem42Input`, `wilkinsonProblem42ExactSum`, `finiteRoundToEvenRecursiveSum_eq_listSum`, `wilkinsonProblem42BlockValue_ieeeDouble_finiteSystem`, `wilkinsonProblem42Input_ieeeDouble_all_finiteSystem`, `wilkinsonProblem42Vector_ieeeDouble_finiteSystem`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_sameBinade_add_rounds_to_nat`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_block_boundary_add_rounds_to_pow`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_block_prefix_accumulator`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_block_rounds_pow_to_next_pow`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_listRecursiveSum_eq_pow`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_finiteRecursiveSum_eq_pow`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_abs_error_eq_defect`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_abs_error_closed_form`, `wilkinsonProblem42_unit_roundoff_le_defect_of_pos`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_first_order_bound_le_three_abs_error_plus_u`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_first_order_bound_le_four_abs_error`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_gamma_bound_le_three_abs_error_plus_u_div`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_gamma_bound_le_eight_abs_error`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_first_block_rounds_to_two`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_finiteRecursiveSum_eq_pow_one`, `wilkinsonProblem42_ieeeDouble_finiteRecursiveSum_eq_pow_two`, `wilkinsonProblem42BlockValue_nonneg_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42Input_nonneg_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42Vector_nonneg_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42Vector_oneSigned_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42Vector_sum_abs_eq`, `wilkinsonProblem42ExactSum_pos_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42ExactSum_ne_zero_of_le`, `wilkinsonProblem42_recursiveSum_running_error_bound`, `wilkinsonProblem42_recursiveSum_forward_error_bound`, `wilkinsonProblem42_recursiveSum_relError_le_gamma`, `wilkinsonProblem42_recursiveSum_relError_le_pow_mul_u` | Formalizes the Higham Chapter 4 Problem 4.2 Wilkinson powers-of-two source family. Lean proves the source length `2^r`, initial singleton, `2^j`-copy block extension by `1 - 2^(j-t)`, exact block-sum formula, and `Fin (2^r)` vector bridge. It proves `exact sum + defect = 2^r`, the closed defect form `3*defect + 2^(-t) = 2^(2*r-t)`, nonnegativity of the defect, `exact sum <= 2^r`, the first-order comparison `(2^r - 1)*2^(-t)*exactSum <= 3*defect + 2^(-t)`, and the exact `gamma (2^r - 1)` denominator comparison when `fp.u = 2^(-t)`. For IEEE double with `t = 53`, Lean proves every displayed input is finite representable, the same-binade and power-boundary step primitives, a full block prefix invariant, the full block map `2^j -> 2^(j+1)`, the arbitrary list and `Fin` finite round-to-even traces `= 2^r` for all `r <= 52`, exact realized absolute error `= defect`, its closed form, first-order near-attainment within factor `4` for positive `r`, and exact-gamma near-attainment within factor `8` under the explicit denominator condition `2*(2^r-1)*2^-53 <= 1`. It also retains the `r = 0`, `r = 1`, and `r = 2` trace footholds and specializes the recursive-summation (4.3), (4.4), gamma-relative, and `n*u` upper bounds to this one-signed family. A fully generic arbitrary-precision `t`-digit version is not claimed; see `docs/CHAPTER04_P42_WILKINSON_ATTAINABILITY_BOTTLENECK.md`. |
| Six-term recursive-summation outputs | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/Problem44SixTerm.lean` | `Problem44Term`, `Problem44Accumulator`, `problem44Step`, `problem44Source`, `problem44Eval`, `problem44Output`, `problem44PossibleOutputs` | `problem44Source_exact_sum`, `problem44PossibleOutputs_eq_Icc`, `problem44Output_mem_Icc_of_perm`, `problem44Every_Icc_output_attained`, `problem44_outputs_exactly_Icc` | Closes Higham Chapter 4 Problem 4.4 in a source-shaped absorbing-large-`M` model. The model has exact small integer additions, loses any small accumulated part when `M` or `-M` is active, and cancels `M` with `-M`; exhaustive enumeration over all six-term permutations proves the possible recursive-summation outputs are exactly `0,1,...,10`, and every value in that interval is attained. The real source multiset sum is separately proved to be `10`. |
| Aitken denominator evaluation | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/AitkenDenominator.lean` | `aitkenDenominatorA`, `aitkenDenominatorB`, `aitkenDenominatorC`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorA`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorB`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorC` | `aitkenDenominatorA_eq_B`, `aitkenDenominatorC_eq_B`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorA_error_bound`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorB_backward_error`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorB_error_bound`, `fl_aitkenDenominatorC_error_bound`, `aitkenDenominatorBMajorant_add_const`, `aitkenDenominator_recommended_route_b` | Closes Higham Chapter 4 Problem 4.6 at the parenthesized standard-model level, with multiplication by `2` treated as exact. Lean proves the three exact expressions are algebraically identical, derives error bounds for the rounded routes, and proves that the recommended first-difference route `(x2-x1)-(x1-x0)` has a majorant depending only on successive differences. That majorant is invariant under adding the common limiting offset, unlike the route (a) and route (c) majorants. |
| Log-product-of-exponentials summation | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LogExpProduct.lean` | `logExpProductExact`, `LogExpProductTrace`, `logExpProductTrace` | `logExpProductExact_eq_sum`, `logExpProduct_product_perturbation`, `logExpProduct_final_error_eq`, `logExpProduct_final_abs_error_eq`, `logExpProduct_final_relError_eq`, `logExpProduct_composed_error` | Closes Higham Chapter 4 Problem 4.7 at the real-valued perturbation surface. Lean proves the exact identity `log (prod_i exp x_i) = sum_i x_i`; proves that `n` relative exponential errors plus a product-stage error bounded by `gamma_{n-1}` combine into one product perturbation bounded by `gamma_{n+(n-1)}`; and proves the final absolute/relative error formula `|log(1+theta)+eta|`, with the relative denominator `|sum_i x_i|`. The final log theorem requires the perturbed product to stay positive, recording the finite no-overflow/no-underflow/no-sign-flip side condition explicitly. |
| Equally spaced grid points | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/GridPoints.lean` | `gridPointExact`, `gridPointFromStep`, `gridPointConvex`, `fl_gridRecurrence`, `fl_gridDirect`, `fl_gridConvex` | `gridPointConvex_eq_exact`, `gridPointFromStep_error_eq`, `fl_gridRecurrence_storedStep_error_bound`, `fl_gridRecurrence_error_bound`, `fl_gridDirect_storedStep_error_bound`, `fl_gridDirect_error_bound`, `fl_gridConvex_error_bound` | Closes Higham Chapter 4 Problem 4.8 at the standard-model grid-point level. Lean proves the convex-combination expression is exactly the `[a,b]` grid point, proves that methods using a stored step `hhat` carry the explicit amplification term `i*|hhat-(b-a)/n|`, and proves rounded-error bounds for recurrence, direct `a+i*hhat`, and convex-combination routes. The convex bound has no stored-step representation term, capturing the source warning that `h` need not be floating point even when `a` and `b` are. |

Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.11. Lean closes the exact real-arithmetic baseline for the MATLAB-shaped routine: after `k+1` square roots of `x^2` and `k` squarings, the result is `|x|`. It defines the concrete finite round-to-even routine surface, proves the initial IEEE-double `x.^2` operation exact for all six source inputs, proves the six source inputs finite in IEEE double, proves the first rounded square root after the exact initial square returns the source input, and reduces the exact remaining Sun `m=75` finite-trace target to the remaining 74 square roots and 74 squares. It also closes the terminal fixed-point pieces needed after the reduced Sun trace reaches the predecessor of one, one, or zero, proves the first thirty predecessor-square cascade transitions `fl((1 - 2^-53)^2) = 1 - 2^-52`, `fl((1 - 2^-52)^2) = 1 - 2^-51`, `fl((1 - 2^-51)^2) = 1 - 2^-50`, `fl((1 - 2^-50)^2) = 1 - 2^-49`, `fl((1 - 2^-49)^2) = 1 - 2^-48`, `fl((1 - 2^-48)^2) = 1 - 2^-47`, `fl((1 - 2^-47)^2) = 1 - 2^-46`, `fl((1 - 2^-46)^2) = 1 - 2^-45`, `fl((1 - 2^-45)^2) = 1 - 2^-44`, `fl((1 - 2^-44)^2) = 1 - 2^-43`, `fl((1 - 2^-43)^2) = 1 - 2^-42`, `fl((1 - 2^-42)^2) = 1 - 2^-41`, `fl((1 - 2^-41)^2) = 1 - 2^-40`, `fl((1 - 2^-40)^2) = 1 - 2^-39`, `fl((1 - 2^-39)^2) = 1 - 2^-38`, `fl((1 - 2^-38)^2) = 1 - 2^-37`, `fl((1 - 2^-37)^2) = 1 - 2^-36`, `fl((1 - 2^-36)^2) = 1 - 2^-35`, `fl((1 - 2^-35)^2) = 1 - 2^-34`, `fl((1 - 2^-34)^2) = 1 - 2^-33`, `fl((1 - 2^-33)^2) = 1 - 2^-32`, `fl((1 - 2^-32)^2) = 1 - 2^-31`, and `fl((1 - 2^-31)^2) = 1 - 2^-30`, `fl((1 - 2^-30)^2) = 1 - 2^-29`, and `fl((1 - 2^-29)^2) = 1 - 2^-28`, and `fl((1 - 2^-28)^2) = 1 - 2^-27`, and `fl((1 - 2^-27)^2) = 1 - 2^-26`, `fl((1 - 2^-26)^2) = 1 - 134217727 * 2^-52`, `fl((1 - 134217727 * 2^-52)^2) = 1 - 536870900 * 2^-53`, and `fl((1 - 536870900 * 2^-53)^2) = 1 - 1073741768 * 2^-53` by the IEEE tie-to-even/nearest-even evidence, and proves the one-step underflow-to-zero terminal theorem once an exact square is strictly below half the smallest subnormal magnitude. It represents all three displayed output rows and proves four-decimal interval certificates for those rows. It also proves two conditional rounded-trace explanations: HP-style threshold phase laws imply the reported Sun SPARCstation `m=75` row `0,0,0,1,1,1` and its display certificate, and all-one phase laws imply the reported 486DX `m=75` row and its display certificate. The concrete below-one/above-one root-hit facts, remaining square-cascade threshold-reach fact from `1 - 1073741768 * 2^-53`, and 486DX `m=50` numerical rounded trace remain open in `docs/CHAPTER03_PROBLEM3_11_BOTTLENECK.md`. |

The remaining square-threshold gap now starts from this thirtieth state. |
| Quadrature with rounded function values | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/Quadrature.lean` | `quadratureRule`, `fl_quadrature` | `fl_quadrature_error_bound_of_function_value_rel_error` | Closes Higham Chapter 3 Problem 3.12 for left-to-right summation: if the function values satisfy `|fhat_i - f_i| <= eta |f_i|`, then the computed quadrature satisfies `|I-fl_quadrature| <= |I-J| + (eta + gamma_n*(1+eta))*sum_i |w_i||f_i|`. The three terms are respectively the analytic quadrature error, the function-evaluation error, and the dot-product rounding error on perturbed values. |

It also closes the source-faithful superoperator trace representation via `cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator_all` and proves the conditional reduction from relative-entropy joint convexity alone to `liebTraceConcavityTarget H` for the chosen Tropp proof route. This is a listed dependency in the Tropp/Lieb proof-source route; it now proves finite-dimensional relative-entropy joint convexity (`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive_all`), arbitrary self-adjoint Lieb trace concavity (`liebTraceConcavityTarget_all`), and the one-step Tropp trace-MGF inequality (`FiniteProbability.expectationReal_trace_normed_exp_add_le`). The scalar Bernstein parabola constants are now proved in the dedicated scalar/CFC row below; this Lieb row itself still does not prove the one-sample matrix-CGF/log-MGF bound, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2). |

`matrix_inv_convex_posDef`, `cstarMatrix_nonneg_of_matrix_posSemidef`, `cstarMatrix_le_of_matrix_le`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_inv_convex_isStrictlyPositive`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_shifted_inv_eq_cfc_inv_add_smul_one`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_shifted_inv_convex_nonneg` | Splits the source route into the ordinary positive-cone matrix-convexity target that Effros uses, the source-faithful all-finite-size version needed by the block proof, the Hansen-Pedersen noncommutative Jensen transfer target, and the assembled two-point Jensen target. The identity-function sanity cases are proved for the ordinary and two-point Jensen shapes, and the concrete `x log x` two-point Jensen theorem is now proved by the reflection-average corner route. The generic all-functions Hansen-Pedersen transfer theorem remains unproved, but it is not used as a hidden hypothesis for the concrete `x log x` theorem. The source-aligned kernel is `x * (x - 1) / (u + (1-u) x)`, not the auxiliary `(x - 1)^2` kernel. The Effros perspective theorem, matrix relative-entropy joint convexity, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein, and equation (2) remain open. |
| C⋆ range-reflection unitary bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/CStarMatrixBridge.lean` | projection reflection as a unitary element | `cstarMatrixProjectionReflection_mem_unitary_of_isSelfAdjoint_of_idempotent`, `cstarMatrixColumnPairRangeReflection_mem_unitary_of_sum` | Registers the already proved reflection \(2P-I\) as a member of mathlib's `unitary` submonoid when \(P=P^*=P^2\), and specializes this to the block-isometry range projection \(P=VV^*\). This is a dependency for a future CFC conjugation/pinching proof; it is not the nonlinear pinching theorem itself. |
| C⋆ unitary-conjugation CFC bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` | CFC under unitary conjugation | `cstarMatrix_cfc_unitary_conj` | Proves \(f(UTU^*) = U f(T)U^*\) for a unitary finite `CStarMatrix` \(U\), self-adjoint \(T\), and \(f\) continuous on the real spectrum of \(T\). This closes the unitary-conjugation CFC substrate for the pinching/reflection route; it does not prove the pinching average, compression Jensen, or the Hansen-Pedersen transfer theorem. |
| C⋆ reflection-pinch CFC bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` | order compression and reflection-average CFC pinching | `cstarMatrix_compression_nonneg`, `cstarMatrix_compression_mono`, `cstarMatrixColumnPair_reflectionAverage_cfc_le_average_of_sum`, `cstarMatrixColumnPair_reflectionAverage_compressed_cfc_le_compressed_of_sum` | Proves that rectangular C⋆-matrix compression preserves nonnegativity and order, applies all-finite ordinary operator convexity to \(D\) and \(RDR\), rewrites \(f(RDR)\) by unitary CFC conjugation, and compresses the resulting reflection-average inequality by the block isometry \(V=[A;B]\). This closes the CFC pinching-inequality side of the Hansen-Pedersen block route. The full Hansen-Pedersen transfer theorem is still open. |
| C⋆ shifted-inverse corner bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/CStarMatrixBridge.lean` and `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` | rectangular unit inverse intertwining and shifted-inverse nonlinear corner identity | `cstarMatrix_units_inv_mul_rect_eq_mul_units_inv_of_mul_eq`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_shifted_inv_mul_rect_eq_mul_cfc_shifted_inv_of_mul_eq`, `cstarMatrixColumnPair_reflectionAverage_shifted_inv_corner_of_sum` | Proves that \(UV=VW\) implies \(U^{-1}V=VW^{-1}\) for rectangular C⋆ products, lifts that algebra to real CFC shifted-inverse kernels, and specializes it to the reflection-average block \(E=(D+RDR)/2\): \(V^*(sI+E)^{-1}V=(sI+V^*DV)^{-1}\). This closes the shifted-inverse subcase used by the direct \(x\log x\) integral route. The full \(x\log x\) corner and concrete two-point Jensen theorem are closed in the separate C⋆ compression and `x log x` corner row; Effros perspective and relative-entropy joint convexity remain open. |
| C⋆ unitary-conjugation positivity bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/CStarMatrixBridge.lean` | strict positivity under unitary conjugation and range reflection | `cstarMatrix_unitary_conj_isStrictlyPositive`, `cstarMatrixColumnPairRangeReflection_conj_isStrictlyPositive_of_sum` | Proves that \(UTU^*\) is strictly positive whenever \(U\) is unitary and \(T\) is strictly positive, and specializes the statement to the range reflection \(2VV^*-I\) under \(V^*V=I\). This closes the domain-preservation side condition for the reflection/pinching route; it is not the nonlinear pinching inequality or Hansen-Pedersen transfer theorem. |
| C⋆ block-compression strict-positivity bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` | strict positivity of Hansen-Pedersen block-isometry compressions | `cstarMatrix_isStrictlyPositive_of_matrix_posDef`, `cstarMatrixColumnPair_mulVec_injective_of_sum`, `cstarMatrixColumnPair_compress_blockDiagonal_isStrictlyPositive_of_sum`, `cstarMatrixHansenPedersenCompression_isStrictlyPositive_of_sum` | Bridges plain matrix positive definiteness back to `CStarMatrix` strict positivity, proves that the block column \(V=[A;B]\) has injective matrix action when \(V^*V=I\), and proves \(V^*\operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2)V\), equivalently \(A^*T_1A+B^*T_2B\), is strictly positive when \(T_1,T_2\) are strictly positive. This closes a compression-domain side condition for the Hansen-Pedersen transfer route; it is not the nonlinear compression/Jensen inequality. |
| Scalar and CFC `x log x` direct kernel bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` | scalar bridge, CFC integral representation, and direct positive-cone operator convexity for `x ↦ x log x` | `real_xlog_eq_sub_one_mul_realNormalizedLogKernel`, `real_xlog_eq_sub_one_mul_normalizedKernel_setIntegral`, `real_xlog_eq_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integral`, `real_unit_interval_xlog_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`, `real_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`, `continuousOn_uncurry_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum`, `real_abs_sub_one_le_max_one_of_pos_le`, `real_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_abs_le_max_sq_of_le`, `real_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max_sq`, `ae_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max_sq`, `hasFiniteIntegral_const_max_one_spectrum_bound_sq`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_xlog_eq_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integral`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_integrand_convex_of_pos_lt_one`, `cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_convex_of_pos_lt_one`, `cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel` | Proves `(x - 1) g(x) = x log x` for the normalized kernel `g = realNormalizedLogKernel`, pushes the factor inside the unit-interval integral to get the corrected kernel `x*(x-1)/(u+(1-u)x)`, proves the scalar/CFC affine-plus-shifted-inverse decompositions, discharges the continuity and boundedness side conditions needed by `cfc_setIntegral`, proves the CFC integral representation of `x log x`, and integrates the pointwise shifted-inverse convexity inequality to close ordinary positive-cone operator convexity of `x ↦ x log x`. The explicit spectrum wrappers say nonnegative C⋆ matrices have nonnegative real spectrum and that `A ≤ cI` implies every real spectral value of `A` is at most `c`. The finite-real adapter says local finite Loewner inequalities become operator-log inequalities after adding a positive scalar identity regularization. This closes regularized log-monotonicity, `log(exp X)=X`, strict positivity of `exp X`, `exp(log A)=A`, and real-spectrum order dependencies for the Tropp/Lieb route, but it does not prove matrix Bernstein or CACM equation (2). |

Bernstein parabola `exp(a*x) <= 1 + a*x + (exp a - a - 1)*x^2` for `a >= 0`, `x <= 1`, its scaled `R > 0` form, the real CFC equality for `x ↦ 1 + theta*x + beta*x^2`, and the explicit Loewner-order CFC lift under a real-spectrum upper bound `x <= R`. It then applies finite-probability C⋆ expectation monotonicity, zero mean, square nonnegativity, strict positivity of exponentials, log monotonicity, and `log(I+B) <= B` to prove the centered one-sample variance proxy `log(E exp(theta • X)) <= g(theta,R) E[X^2]`, including support-aware variants where the spectral bound is required only on positive-probability atoms. The scalar concentration utilities also include the exact `T = log(2B/delta)` failure-budget simplification, the Bennett optimizer `theta = log(1 + L*r/W)/L`, fully proved bridges from quadratic denominators `2W+Lr` and `2W+(2/3)Lr` to the Bennett budget, and a one-sided `δ/2` tail-budget theorem from a Bennett sample-size inequality. The source-aligned square Algorithm 1 constants and the Algorithm 2 leverage finite-Loewner sample-budget theorem now reuse these scalar bridges. |

The deterministic radius simplifies to \(T_U^{fp}=(gamma_s(1+lambda_U)^2+2lambda_U+lambda_U^2) * sum_j (sum_{i:p_i>0} |U_ij|/sqrt(p_i))^2\), where `lambda_U = 2*u + u^2 + (1+u)^2 * sum_{i:p_i>0} sqrt(s*p_i)*(sqrt(1+u)*u+u)/|fl_sqrt(fl_mul s p_i)|`. Its fixed-problem small-roundoff order is `Theta((gamma_s + u*(1 + support_size)) * column_factor)`, and it tends to zero as `u,gamma_s -> 0`. QR/SVD/basis-generation from an input matrix remains a separate `ComputedMatrix` routine obligation. |

random-projection/preconditioning meta-algorithm. The formalization is deterministic after the random preprocessing matrices have been drawn. It proves exact Frobenius preservation for square orthogonal preprocessors, preserves the `HasOrthonormalColumns` leverage-basis invariant and equation (6) denominator under square orthogonal row/column/two-sided preprocessing, closes the deterministic signed-diagonal/Hadamard-style orthogonality prerequisite for the SRHT route, adds the uniform finite Rademacher sign-vector law, proves finite Rademacher first/second moment identities, exact signed-linear-form MGF factorization, scalar Hoeffding MGF/tail bounds, introduces the flat Hadamard-style side condition, proves expected signed-Hadamard preconditioned row norm squared `n / m`, proves the expected Euclidean row-norm bound, packages the deterministic signed-Hadamard row-norm convexity and Lipschitz inputs, proves the positive-flip self-bounding estimate, proves the source-sharp finite-cube exponential-tilt bridge, derives the one-row `exp(-m t^2/8)` row-norm tail, lifts it to all rows, converts it to equation-(6) leverage probabilities, proves the closed logarithmic choice `t = sqrt(8 * log(m / δ) / m)`, composes the sharper preprocessing event with iid uniform row-sampling matrix concentration on the product law, and transfers the source-sharp exact event through the rounded uniform-sketch Gram computation with the fixed row-norm-derived FP budget. It also retains the weak Markov/union and coordinate-Hoeffding alternatives. |

A computed Hadamard/FHT table and computed realized sign vector are lifted to the rounded preconditioner `fl(Hhat * diag(signhat))`, with an entrywise budget charging product roundoff, transform-table error, and sign-storage error. The exact-factor specialization assumes exact supplied Hadamard/sign factors and charges only the rounded product forming `H * diag(sign)`. `ComputedMatrix.flSqrtInvNatScaledPattern` closes the rounded scale-factor subcase for a supplied sign-pattern table by charging `fl_sqrt ((m : R)^-1)` entrywise, `hadamardFlat_sqrt_inv_nat_mul_signPattern` proves the ideal scaled sign pattern satisfies `HadamardFlat`, and `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPattern` plugs that rounded scaled table into the signed-diagonal product certificate. `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern` closes the generated sign-pattern subcase for dimensions `2^p`: exact bit-parity logic supplies the `±1` table, `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern_isSignPattern` proves the sign-pattern condition, the parity-count sign adapters convert future equal-count or plus-one partner-row count identities into same-sign or negated-sign table entries, and the finite-sum stage-bit adapter proves the abstract case where two row indices agree off the stage bit and toggle that bit from cleared to set, and the concrete lower/upper partner recurrences instantiate it for generated `i + 2^stage` and `i - 2^stage` partners. `ComputedMatrix.flSqrtInvNatScaledSylvesterPattern` charges only the rounded scale table for that generated pattern. `ComputedVector.flStoredSign` closes one concrete non-exact sign-storage path: storing an absolute-one realized sign by `fl_mul sign_i 1` has entry radius `u`, and `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSign` feeds that stored-sign diagonal into the same rounded-product certificate. `ComputedVector.flStoredSignAddZeroRight` closes the analogous `fl_add sign_i 0` copy path, and `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSignAddZeroRight` feeds that stored-sign diagonal into the same rounded-product certificate. `ComputedVector.flStoredSignSubZeroRight` closes the analogous `fl_sub sign_i 0` copy path, and `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSignSubZeroRight` feeds that stored-sign diagonal into the same rounded-product certificate. The Rademacher sign law remains exact by convention; this row only charges arithmetic/storage after the exact sign outcome is realized. A fast in-place FHT recurrence/apply routine and sign-storage formats other than the three modeled rounded-copy paths still need their own certificates. |

p)`, so membership is equivalent to existence of a stage `stage < p` whose pair obeys `b = a + 2^stage` and `a mod (2 * 2^stage) < 2^stage`. The vector and columnwise-matrix theorems specialize the generic propagated FHT FP budget to this generated full schedule, and the computed-matrix constructor uses the rounded `fl_sqrt ((2^p : R)^-1)` normalization. The stage-factorization bridge proves that the flat generated list, rounded flat list, and propagated flat-list budget are exactly the corresponding stage-by-stage recurrences over `List.range p`; the stage-list append and `List.range (stage+1)` recurrences give the exact, rounded, and propagated-budget induction hook for applying all previous stages followed by the current stage. The exact one-stage stage-output formula is closed by `fhtSylvesterStageScheduleExact_apply_pair_of_mem`; the explicit-hypothesis lower/upper coordinate formulas are closed by `fhtSylvesterStageScheduleExact_apply_lower_mk` and `fhtSylvesterStageScheduleExact_apply_upper_mk`, and the power-of-two block-test wrappers `fhtSylvesterStageScheduleExact_apply_lower_of_mod_lt` and `fhtSylvesterStageScheduleExact_apply_upper_of_mod_ge` derive the needed partner bounds from the lower/upper modulus test. The bit-test wrappers `fhtSylvesterStage_testBit_eq_false_of_mod_lt` and `fhtSylvesterStage_testBit_eq_true_of_mod_ge` identify the same lower/upper tests with the cleared/set stage bit of the coordinate index, and `fhtSylvesterStage_upper_partner_testBit_eq_true_of_mod_lt` plus `fhtSylvesterStage_lower_partner_testBit_eq_false_of_mod_ge` give the matching set/cleared bit facts for the generated partner coordinates. The generic add/sub bit-toggle lemmas and the stage wrappers `fhtSylvesterStage_upper_partner_testBit_eq_of_ne_of_mod_lt` and `fhtSylvesterStage_lower_partner_testBit_eq_of_ne_of_mod_ge` prove non-stage-bit agreement for those partners. The parity-count sign adapters `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern_eq_or_neg_of_parityWeight_eq_add_bool`, `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern_eq_of_parityWeight_eq`, and `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern_neg_of_parityWeight_eq_add_one` turn a count identity into the same-sign or negated-sign recurrence; `sylvesterHadamardParityWeight_partner_eq_add_stage_of_bits` and `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern_partner_eq_or_neg_of_stage_bit` close the abstract finite-sum recurrence when a partner agrees off the stage bit and toggles the stage bit from false to true; the four concrete lower/upper partner recurrences instantiate this for generated block-test partners. The partial-transform target is named by `sylvesterHadamardPartialUnscaledApply`: the zero-depth theorem is the identity transform and the full-depth theorem is the full `sylvesterHadamardUnscaledApply`; the transform-correctness target is named by `fhtSylvesterScheduleRealizesSignPattern`; the zero-stage base case, one-stage partial recurrence, and final range induction are closed, and the unconditional signed-input theorem identifies the scaled generated FHT with the exact `H D U` preconditioner used by the SRHT analysis. This closes generated-schedule FP propagation, stage decomposition, the stage-list append/range-succ induction spine, the partial-transform base/full-depth anchors, the realization base case, the coordinate coverage wrappers, generated-partner stage-bit and non-stage-bit wrappers, the abstract and concrete finite-sum bit-toggle/sign adapters, the one-stage partial parity-table recurrence, the final exact generated-FHT realization induction, and the unconditional exact preconditioner bridge; still open are layout/storage/overwrite effects for concrete array routines beyond the functional fast `ComputedPreconditioner` path. |

| RandNLA fast generated Sylvester FHT preconditioner | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | `ComputedMatrix.flRowSignMul`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardSylvesterFhtSchedule`, `signedHadamardSylvesterFhtSchedulePreconditioner`, `signedHadamardSylvesterFhtScheduleStoredSignPreconditioner` | `ComputedMatrix.flRowSignMul_entry_error_bound`, `fhtScaledSylvesterScheduleMatrixExact_diag_eq_matMul_diag`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardSylvesterFhtSchedule_entry_error_bound`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterFhtScheduleComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterFhtScheduleStoredSignComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one` | Functional fast-FHT implementation-facing path for Algorithm 3. A computed diagonal sign matrix is fed through the rounded generated Sylvester/Walsh FHT schedule with rounded `sqrt(1/2^p)` normalization, producing a `ComputedPreconditioner` for the ideal scaled `H D_omega` matrix. The exact Rademacher and uniform-row laws are unchanged; the budget charges sign storage or exact signs, diagonal input error, butterfly arithmetic, rounded scale, `Vhat` formation, row scaling, and Gram dot products. | Concrete Algorithm 3 FHT storage/copy certificates for the modeled routine that applies a rounded pair-update schedule and writes only the two modified output coordinates through `fl_add(output,0)`, `fl_mul(output,1)`, or `fl_sub(output,0)`. The add-zero, multiply-one, and subtract-zero routes are promoted to final actual-input concrete-denominator SRHT theorems: exact Rademacher and uniform-row laws remain unchanged, while the budget charges butterfly arithmetic, propagated input error, the indicator writeback/copy term for `fl_add(output,0)`, `fl_mul(output,1)`, or `fl_sub(output,0)` only on modified coordinates, rounded sqrt-inverse scale, computed preconditioner formation, `Vhat = fl(Pihat*A)`, concrete denominator formation, row scaling, and Gram dot products. The same-stage no-alias and list-order behavior for distinct generated pairs is closed by the rounded and modified-coordinate commutation lemmas above. The no-touch schedule lemmas prove that coordinates outside every scheduled pair are carried exactly by the base rounded and modified-coordinate schedules, and the matching propagated-budget lemmas prove that those entries keep exactly their incoming error radius, with no hidden all-coordinate copy term. Remaining FHT implementation work is limited to in-place overwrite, vectorized layout, or storage routines whose behavior differs from this modified-coordinate writeback model. |
| RandNLA computed preprocessed SRHT/uniform-row transfers | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | `signedHadamardComputedPreconditionedFlUniformRowSampleGramTwoSidedEvent`, `signedHadamardComputedPreconditionedFlUniformRowPerturbEvent`, implemented preprocessed basis `Vhat` | `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_of_exact_event`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_srht`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget_srht`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget_srht_log_preprocess` | Computed-`\hat V` correction layer for PDF Corollaries 171--173. Uniform row sampling still uses the exact uniform law; there is no computed leverage-probability table in these corollaries. The hidden computation is the implemented preprocessed basis. These theorems transport the exact signed-Hadamard/uniform-row event to an arbitrary implemented `Vhat`, subtracting an additional `delta_comp` and adding a perturbation event that must charge Hadamard/sign arithmetic, computed or stored basis vectors, computed uniform scale denominators, rounded row scaling, and rounded Gram dot products. |
| RandNLA Algorithm 3 actual-input SRHT concrete-denominator endpoint | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | exact analysis factorization `A = U C`, `rightGramCongruence`, `signedHadamardUniformRowFactoredInputSampleGramTwoSidedEvent`, computed `fl(Pihat * A)`, concrete denominator `fl_mul (fl_sqrt s) (fl_div 1 (fl_sqrt m))`, `fl_uniformRowSampleGramDotWithComputedDen`, sample-dependent concrete FP budget | `rightGramCongruence`, `uniformRowSampleGram_factoredInput_error_eq_rightGramCongruence_error`, `signedHadamardUniformRowSampleGramTwoSidedEvent_subset_factoredInput`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_uniformRowFactoredInputSampleGram_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_srht_log_preprocess`, `uniformRowUnitRoundoff_lt_one_of_pos_gammaValid`, `uniformRowFlSqrtMulInvSqrtScaleDen`, `uniformRowFlSqrtMulInvSqrtScaleDen_den_abs_error`, `signedHadamardComputedPreconditionedFactoredInputFlUniformRowSampleGramWithComputedDenTwoSidedEvent`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedPreconditioned_factoredInput_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDotWithComputedDen_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_of_exact_event`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignComputedLeftPreconditioned_factoredInput_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDotWithComputedDen_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_srht_log_preprocess`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignComputedLeftPreconditioned_factoredInput_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDotWithFlSqrtMulInvSqrtDen_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_srht_log_preprocess` | Final Algorithm 3 SRHT path for the actual input matrix, not an assumed orthonormal input. The theorem uses exact analysis-only factors `U,C` with `A=U C`; the algorithm computes `Yhat=fl(Pihat * A)`, computes the uniform denominator as `fl_mul (fl_sqrt (s : R)) (fl_div 1 (fl_sqrt (m : R)))` with relative radius `(4u + 3u^2 + u^3)/(1-u)`, rounds sampled-row divisions, and rounds Gram dot products. Exact Rademacher and uniform-row laws remain exact; no `delta_comp`, perturbation-event hypothesis, certificate-existence assumption, or generic denominator parameter remains in the final concrete endpoint. The generic `WithComputedDen` theorem is retained as infrastructure for other denominator routines. Open follow-ons are non-SRHT transform laws, concrete QR/SVD routines if the analysis factors themselves must be generated as algorithm outputs, and layout-specific FHT variants not represented by the existing functional schedules. |
| RandNLA computed-left general input `A` bound | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | `matrixColumnAbsSum`, `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftEntryErrorBudget` | `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftEntryErrorBudget_le_columnAbsSum_of_uniform_bounds`, `fl_preconditionRowsWithComputedLeft_entry_error_bound_of_uniform_preconditioner_bounds` | Exact-input Algorithm 3 row-preconditioning specialization for a general matrix `A`, not an orthonormal analysis basis. It collapses the raw computed-left budget for `fl(Pihat * A) - Pi * A` to `(gamma_m * phat + alpha) * sum_k |A_kj|` when the computed preconditioner has uniform entry magnitude `phat` and entry-error radius `alpha`. This is the library surface used by the Algorithm 3 theorem sheet's new `A`-input result. |

`signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignAddZeroRightComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignSubZeroRightComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterPatternComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterPatternStoredSignComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterPatternStoredSignAddZeroRightComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_sylvesterPatternStoredSignSubZeroRightComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one` | Concrete certificate instantiation for the Algorithm 3 computed-`\hat V` perturbation event when the implementation forms `Vhat(ω) = fl(Pihat(ω) * U)` from a `ComputedPreconditioner` certificate for the ideal signed-Hadamard matrix `H D_ω`. The generic theorem proves the perturbation event with probability one under the joint exact Rademacher/uniform-row law, using a visible budget that adds rounded row scaling and Gram-dot-product error after `Vhat` is formed plus exact sampled-Gram perturbation from `Vhat - H D_ω U`. The exact-stored specialization instantiates `Pihat` by `ComputedPreconditioner.exact`, so the transform-storage term is zero and the remaining budget charges rounded formation of `Vhat`, row scaling, and Gram dot products. The exact-factor specialization charges the rounded formation of `H D_ω` from exact supplied Hadamard/sign factors before the same computed-left `Vhat` event. The scaled-pattern specialization charges rounded `sqrt(1/m)` table formation, signed-preconditioner formation, `Vhat` formation, row scaling, and Gram dot products while preserving the exact Rademacher/uniform-row law. The stored-sign scaled-pattern specializations additionally charge rounded storage of realized Rademacher signs by `fl_mul sign_i 1`, `fl_add sign_i 0`, or `fl_sub sign_i 0`. The Sylvester/Walsh generated-pattern specializations use exact bit-parity signs in dimension `2^p`, then charge rounded scale formation, optional sign storage, `H D_omega`, `Vhat`, row scaling, and Gram dot products under the same exact probability laws. If the input basis is itself computed, use the computed-input row below. |
| RandNLA computed-input-basis `Vhat` instantiation | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | `ComputedMatrix`, `fl_preconditionRowsWithComputedLeftAndInput`, `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftInputEntryErrorBudget`, `signedHadamardComputedLeftInputPreconditionedBasis`, `signedHadamardComputedLeftInputUniformRowPerturbBudget` | `ComputedMatrix.entry_abs_error_bound`, `ComputedMatrix.exact`, `preconditionRows_computedInput_entry_error_bound`, `preconditionRows_computedLeft_input_entry_error_bound`, `fl_preconditionRowsWithComputedLeftAndInput_error_bound`, `fl_preconditionRowsWithComputedLeftAndInput_total_error_bound`, `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftInputEntryErrorBudget_nonneg`, `fl_preconditionRowsWithComputedLeftInput_entry_error_budget_bound`, `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftInputEntryErrorBudget_exactInput`, `flPreconditionRowsWithComputedLeftInputEntryErrorBudget_exact`, `signedHadamardComputedLeftInputPreconditionedBasisEntryErrorBudget_exactInput`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_computedLeftInputPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one` | Concrete certificate instantiation for Algorithm 3 when the basis or singular-vector table fed into preprocessing is itself computed. `ComputedMatrix` records entrywise `Uhat - U` bounds. The deterministic preconditioning theorem bounds `fl(Pihat * Uhat) - Pi * U` by three visible terms: rounded matrix multiplication, computed preconditioner entry error, and computed input-basis entry error. The signed-Hadamard probability-one theorem plugs that entry budget into the existing exact Rademacher/uniform-row law without changing any sampling probabilities. Concrete SVD/QR/basis-generation routines still need to instantiate `ComputedMatrix`; this row supplies the transfer theorem once such a certificate is available. |

The projector budget is `gamma_k * sum |Qstore_i a||Qstore_j a| + sum (C_i a + E_i a)|Qstore_j a| + sum |Q_i a|(C_j a + E_j a)`, and the two-sided theorem plugs left and right generated-then-stored projectors into `fl(PiLhat A PiRhat)`. Their actual-unit-roundoff siblings specialize `Ucap = fp.u` and derive the local, triangular, or sampled `gammaValid` guards internally from `(m : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` or `(s : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`; the stored-lower actual-unit siblings also derive raw source-denominator nonbreakdown from the stored recurrence, signed-alpha definition, and local diagonal dominance. The concrete-dual siblings use `storedQRSignedStage_normSqBudget_of_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_diagDominant_concreteDualProductSequenceBudget` to avoid exposing auxiliary `κ`, `K`, and dual compact-budget fields on this active branch. `SumTree.forward_error`, and the generic relative-error corollaries are in the same namespace and have generic names. `RecursiveSum.lean` records ordinary recursive summation and the Section 4.6 higher-precision recursive-sum-then-round trace, parameterized by an explicit final working-precision rounding map; it does not claim the Section 4.6 mixed-precision error bound. `InsertionSum.lean` records the general source-level insertion loop over an active list ordered by increasing absolute value: `insertIncreasingAbs` preserves the invariant and nonemptiness, a nonterminal `insertionStep` removes the two smallest active entries, adds them, reinserts the rounded sum, `insertionActiveAfter_preserves_increasingAbs` preserves sortedness through repeated steps, `insertionActiveAfter_ne_nil_of_ne_nil` preserves nonemptiness through repeated steps, `insertionActiveAfter_full_length_le_one` proves that `xs.length` fuel reaches a terminal active list of length at most one, `insertionActiveAfter_full_length_eq_one_of_ne_nil` proves that nonempty input reaches exactly one active value, `insertionActiveAfter_full_eq_singleton_of_ne_nil` exposes that terminal singleton, and `fl_insertionSumList_eq_terminal_singleton_of_ne_nil` proves that the source-level sum returns it. The list-shaped schedule layer records active binary trees, proves that the schedule trace projects to the source active-list values, preserves represented source leaves up to `List.Perm`, reaches a singleton schedule item for nonempty input, `fl_insertionSumList_has_list_schedule_of_ne_nil` represents the final insertion sum by a binary tree whose leaves permute the original active list, and `fl_insertionSumList_has_sumTree_shape_of_ne_nil` packages the dependent `SumTree` shape, `InsertionScheduleTree.leafVector_eq_leaves_get` identifies the `Fin` source vector with the concrete leaf list, and `fl_insertionSumList_has_sumTree_eval_of_ne_nil` proves the insertion result is exactly that Algorithm 4.1 `SumTree.eval`. `InsertionScheduleTree.exactMergeCost` records the exact absolute-intermediate-sum objective, `InsertionScheduleTree.exactMergeCost_node_of_nonnegative` removes the absolute value at nonnegative merge nodes, and `InsertionScheduleTree.toSumTree_runningErrorBudget_exactWithUnitRoundoff` identifies the converted `SumTree.runningErrorBudget` with that cost under exact arithmetic. The local one-step nonnegative insertion choice is now closed by `insertion_first_two_exact_sum_le_pair_sum_of_nonnegative`; the remaining insertion item in Chapter 4 is the global nonnegative optimal-merge theorem. It also records the two p. 89 insertion examples: `1,2,4,8` reduces to the recursive parenthesization, and `1,1+eps,1+2eps,1+3eps` with `0 < eps < 1/2` reduces to four-input pairwise summation. Those examples also inherit the source-facing inverse-model running-error bound and one-signed exact-`gamma` relative-error corollaries. `OrderingExamples.lean` defines weak and strict `IncreasingMagnitudeOrder`/`DecreasingMagnitudeOrder` surfaces for explicit finite permutations, strict-to-weak bridges, `fl_recursiveSumInOrder`, and `sum_orderedInput_eq_sum`; it also records Higham's p. 91 example (4.5) under an explicit abstract rounding certificate: increasing and Psum orders compute zero with relative error one, decreasing order computes the exact sum, and the displayed running-error budgets are `4M`, `3M`, and `M+1`. `CompensatedSum.lean` records the rounded correction-formula trace `s = fl(a + b); e = fl((a - s) + b)` behind equation (4.7), but does not claim the finite base-2 exactness theorem. It also records a local no-guard correction-formula trace and a concrete `u = 1/4`, `a = 1`, `b = -7/8` local model witness with `|a| > |b|` where `a + b = s + e` is false. It records Kahan Algorithm 4.2's state, step trace, prefix trace, final sum, final correction, and the p. 93 final-correction variant `s = s + e`, including the displayed evaluation order for `e = (temp - s) + y`; it does not claim the bounds (4.8)--(4.9) or the stronger final-correction bound. It also records the p. 94 alternative compensated-summation trace: local corrections are stored separately, recursively summed, and then added to the computed main sum; it does not claim the equation (4.10) bound. It also records Kahan's modified no-guard correction over `NoGuardFPModel`, including `f = 0`, the same-sign `f = (0.46*s - s) + s` branch, and `e = ((temp - f) - (s - f)) + y`; it does not claim the compensated-summation no-guard failure for (4.9) or Kahan's machine-dependent guarantee. `AccumulatorSum.lean` records Wolfe/Malcolm/Ross-style accumulator banks with an abstract machine-dependent overflow predicate, reset-and-carry cascade, final recursive summation in a supplied decreasing-absolute-value order, and a Kahan-style distillation trace that preserves the exact sum and terminates when the final component has relative error at most `u`; it does not claim Malcolm's order-`u` error bound or distillation runtime estimates. `DoublyCompensatedSum.lean` records Priest Algorithm 4.3's weak and strict decreasing-magnitude input predicates, strict-to-weak bridge, state, step trace, prefix trace, final sum, and final correction; it does not claim Priest's full-precision guarantee. The source-shaped carry-forward route matches Higham p. 88 by carrying the unpaired odd entry to the next stage and proves depth `Nat.clog 2 n` and radius `gamma (ceil(log2 n))`; the scalar padded route uses the same `Nat.clog 2 n` radius after exact zero-padding. `fl_pairwiseSumSixDisplayed_eq` separately records the source's displayed `n = 6` parenthesization. The one-signed corollaries are exact `gamma` statements under nonzero exact-sum hypotheses. |

| Algorithm 4.1 materialization into insertion schedules | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/InsertionSum.lean` | arbitrary dependent `SumTree n` plus source vector; list-shaped `InsertionScheduleTree` bridge | `SumTree.toInsertionScheduleTree`, `SumTree.toInsertionScheduleTree_eval`, `SumTree.toInsertionScheduleTree_exactEval`, `SumTree.toInsertionScheduleTree_leafCount`, `SumTree.toInsertionScheduleTree_leaves_nonnegative`, `SumTree.eval_exactWithUnitRoundoff`, `SumTree.runningErrorBudget_exactWithUnitRoundoff_eq_toInsertionScheduleTree_exactMergeCost`, `SumTree.runningErrorBudget_exactWithUnitRoundoff_eq_weightedLeafDepthCost_of_nonnegative` | Any Algorithm 4.1 dependent `SumTree` with a concrete input vector can be materialized as a list-shaped insertion schedule tree. The bridge preserves leaf count, floating-point evaluation, exact evaluation, and under exact arithmetic identifies the original `SumTree.runningErrorBudget` with the materialized tree's exact merge cost. For nonnegative source vectors it also transfers nonnegativity to all materialized leaves and rewrites the exact-arithmetic running-error budget directly as weighted external path length. This closes the comparison-surface bridge needed before stating p. 91 global optimality over all Algorithm 4.1 schedules. |

| Insertion same-shape relabeling realization | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/InsertionSum.lean` | list-shaped insertion schedule trees and explicit leaf-depth/weight pair lists | `InsertionScheduleTree.relabelLeaves`, `InsertionScheduleTree.relabelLeaves_leaves_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.relabelLeaves_leafDepthWeights_eq_zip`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_relabelLeaves_leafDepthWeights_eq_of_depths_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_relabelLeaves_for_two_smallest_deepest_exchange_branch_of_depths_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_deepest_pair_noop_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_reverse_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_before_deepest_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_reverse_before_deepest_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_around_deepest_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_two_pair_exchange_reverse_around_deepest_of_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_left_deepest_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_right_deepest_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_left_deepest_second_two_smallest_decomposition_eq`, `InsertionScheduleTree.exists_tree_for_right_deepest_second_two_smallest_decomposition_eq` | A target explicit leaf-depth/weight list with exactly the same depth sequence as an existing tree is realized by relabeling that tree's leaves. The branch-specific adapters instantiate this for the already-arranged no-op branch, all six nonoverlap orderings of the deepest-pair block and the two selected slots, and all four one-slot overlap cases when the original `leafDepthWeights` is displayed by equality in the corresponding context. |
| Insertion separated weighted-depth exchange | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/InsertionSum.lean` | explicit leaf-depth/weight pair lists | `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_separated_le`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_separated_of_perm_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_of_context_min_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_of_left_deepest_two_smallest_decomposition_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_of_right_deepest_two_smallest_decomposition_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_of_left_deepest_second_two_smallest_decomposition_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_exchange_of_right_deepest_second_two_smallest_decomposition_le_and_weights_perm` | If `a <= b` and one selected leaf-depth position is no shallower than another, swapping `a` into the deeper separated position cannot increase the explicit weighted-depth cost. The bundled permutation/context-minimum variants also preserve the leaf-weight multiset and derive the needed `a <= b` inequality from a residual context. The left/right deepest overlap branches instantiate this one-slot exchange when either the first or second two-smallest entry is already one member of the deepest sibling pair and the other selected entry must be moved into the remaining deepest slot. |
| Insertion two-slot weighted-depth exchange | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/InsertionSum.lean` | explicit leaf-depth/weight pair lists | `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_pair_exchange_separated_le`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_pair_exchange_separated_of_perm_le`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_pair_exchange_of_context_min_le`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_pair_exchange_of_context_min_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_pair_exchange_of_two_smallest_decomposition_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.TwoSmallestDeepestExchangeBranch`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_two_smallest_deepest_exchange_branch_le_and_weights_perm`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedLeafDepthCost_two_smallest_deepest_exchange_branch_realized_le_and_leaves_perm` | If `a <= c` and `b <= d`, and two selected source slots are no deeper than adjacent deepest slots, replacing the deepest weights `c, d` by `a, b` cannot increase explicit weighted-depth cost. The permutation wrapper transfers the inequality through arbitrary pair-list rearrangements with `List.Perm`; the context-minimum handoff derives the required `a <= c` and `b <= d` inequalities from a decomposed residual context whose weights are all at least `b`. The bundled handoff additionally proves that the exchange preserves the multiset of leaf weights, giving the next tree-assembly route both the cost inequality and same-input invariant. The nondegenerate two-smallest/deepest-pair branch derives the residual context-minimum hypothesis from a finite two-smallest decomposition when the two selected slots are displayed outside the deepest pair. The dispatcher packages no-op, nonoverlap, and all one-slot overlap cases behind one reusable pair-list theorem; the realized-tree bridge lifts any realized dispatcher output back to weighted external path length and schedule leaves. |
| Insertion sibling weighted-depth contraction | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/InsertionSum.lean` | weighted external path length and explicit leaf-depth/weight pair lists | `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedLeafDepthCost_node_eq_children_at_depth_add_exactEval`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedLeafDepthCost_node_leaf_leaf_eq_contract`, `InsertionScheduleTree.weightedDepthPairsCost_pair_contract_eq` | Expanding a node by one level adds one copy of each child's exact value to weighted external path length. For sibling leaves, contracting the two leaves into their parent weight removes exactly one copy of the merged weight; the explicit pair-list version states the same identity for a local sibling pair inside arbitrary prefix/suffix context. This is the contraction identity needed by the p. 91 optimal-merge induction route. |

It proves the top-block QR normal-equation handoff, pulls top-block perturbations through a common orthogonal factor, and lets supplied common-`Q`, supplied orthogonal-sequence, compact Householder, and stored trailing Householder routes feed the local `LSQRSolveBackwardError` specification under explicit norm-budget hypotheses. The stored-loop handoff derives the final transformed `[R;0]` shape locally; strengthened variants derive nonzero final diagonal entries from `budget_k < |alpha_k|`, reduce the Householder denominator side condition to `A_hat[k,k] != alpha_k`, reduce that scalar condition to sign choice plus positive trailing-column norm, specialize that sign choice to the concrete `signedHouseholderAlpha` rule, push the prefix-span plus active-entry-budget route into the solver-facing certificate, replace that active-entry witness by the dimensioned norm-square margin `m * budget_k^2 < ||A_k(k:m,k)||_2^2`, replace that norm-square margin by a leading-dual norm budget `||L_k(last,:)||_2^2 <= K_k` plus `m * budget_k^2 < 1/K_k`, replace the ambient dual-row norm by a local leading-block inverse row-norm budget, replace that row budget by a local inverse Frobenius-norm budget, replace the Frobenius budget by a local inverse infinity-norm budget `(k+1)||C_k||_∞^2 <= K_k`, use `det S_k != 0` to instantiate that inverse-∞ route with `C_k = nonsingInv S_k` when the same inverse-norm budget is visible, derive that inverse-∞ budget from a local `κ∞` bound plus a positive lower bound on `||S_k||∞` when those conditioning assumptions are supplied, specialize the same route with `rho_k = ||S_k||∞` using determinant positivity to remove the separate lower-bound parameter, replace the inverse-∞ budget by Higham's diagonal-dominant triangular estimate when those stronger domain assumptions are supplied, remove the explicit inverse witness from that diagonal-dominant route under `det S_k != 0`, push the nonsingular-leading-block plus square-root-budget route into the solver-facing certificate, provide an explicit final-budget wrapper using `qrSolveFinalGramBudget`/`qrSolveFinalRhsBudget`, and provide an explicit compact-budget wrapper using `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget`. Thus the final Gram/RHS and compact-update domination hypotheses are no longer separate user assumptions in the prefix-local signed-alpha route. |

Builds the leading dual used by the source-faithful route by zero-padding a row of a local left inverse of the current leading block, then chooses the repository final Gram/RHS radii and stored compact-update sequence budget. The Frobenius and infinity wrappers compose the existing inverse-norm bridges with the same repository-budget choices. The latest stored-prefix-span wrappers derive `qrPrefixSupportSpannedByPreviousColumns` from the actual stored panel recurrence plus local left inverses of the previous leading blocks, so the row, Frobenius, and infinity compact-budget routes no longer assume prefix-span separately. The signed-alpha row, Frobenius, and infinity wrappers further derive the squared-alpha and sign-choice hypotheses from `signedHouseholderAlpha`. The signed-alpha determinant companions instantiate the previous/current local left-inverse witnesses with `nonsingInv` from nonzero determinants of the previous transposed leading block and current leading block, while preserving the visible row/Frobenius/infinity inverse-budget and dual compact-smallness assumptions. The `κ∞` companion derives the direct inverse-∞ budget from a visible local condition-number bound and self-norm squared budget. The triangular companion derives the determinant facts from visible upper-triangular leading shape and nonzero leading diagonal entries. This closes the local-dual-construction, budget-instantiation, prefix-span-from-stored-lower-zero, sign-choice, local-left-inverse-witness, direct inverse-∞ budget, and determinant-from-triangular-shape dependencies under visible triangular, diagonal, `κ∞`, and compact-smallness hypotheses. It still does not derive the triangular/nonzero-diagonal invariant, condition-number budgets, or compact-smallness from a computed-loop/conditioning invariant. |

The explicit-compact specialization also chooses the compact-update domination constant as `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget`, removing separate panel/RHS compact-update domination hypotheses. `rect_orthogonal_sequence_geometric`, `orthogonal_vector_sequence_geometric`, `rect_orthogonal_matrix_vector_sequence_geometric`, `householder_qr_backward`, `givens_qr_backward`, `qr_solve_backward_from_components`, `qr_solve_perturbation_bound` | Mostly contract/interface level. The zero-prefix and active-vector lemmas prove exact embedded Householder behavior. The QR-specific trailing active-vector lemmas handle the standard rectangular QR reflector, sign-choice denominator nonbreakdown, and exact lower-trapezoidal shape. The scalar lower-bound bridges convert a nonzero active trailing entry into `0 < ||x_tail||_2^2`, record that this positive trailing norm does not imply the current unpivoted pivot is nonzero, show that a nonnegative budget strictly below `sqrt(||x_tail||_2^2)` already supplies positive trailing norm, turn a concrete active-entry magnitude lower bound into the square-root trailing-norm budget, prove that `m * budget_k^2 < ||x_tail||_2^2` supplies such an active-entry lower bound, and rewrite that square-root budget as `budget_k < |alpha_k|`. The leading-dual norm bridge proves that prefix-span plus a dual row with `||L_last||_2^2 <= K` gives `1 / K <= ||x_tail||_2^2`, and converts `m * budget_k^2 < 1/K` into the dimensioned norm-square margin. The local-inverse row bridge proves that zero-padding a row of the leading-block left inverse preserves squared norm, so the same margin can be derived from a local inverse row-norm budget; the Frobenius inverse-norm bridge uses `vecNorm2Sq_row_le_frobNorm_sq` to replace that row budget by `||C_k||_F^2 <= K_k`; the infinity-norm bridge uses `frobNorm_sq_le_nat_mul_infNorm_sq` to replace the Frobenius budget by `(k+1)||C_k||_∞^2 <= K_k`. The prefix-span bridge defines `qrColumnNotInPreviousSpan` and `qrPrefixSupportSpannedByPreviousColumns`; `qrPreviousLeadingBlockTranspose` and `qrPrefixBasisCoefficientMatrix_of_leftInverse_previousLeadingBlockTranspose` reuse the local `IsLeftInverse` predicate to produce concrete prefix-basis coefficients, and `qrPrefixSupportSpannedByPreviousColumns_of_prefixBasisCoefficientMatrix` turns those coefficients plus the QR lower-zero shape into the prefix-span invariant. The stored-loop prefix-span wrappers use this with sign choice and either a square-root trailing-norm budget, a concrete active-entry magnitude budget, the dimensioned norm-square budget, a leading-dual norm budget, a local leading-block inverse row-norm budget, a local inverse Frobenius-norm budget, or a local inverse infinity-norm budget to prove final nonzero diagonals. `qrLeadingBlock` and `qrLeadingColumnLeftInverse_of_leftInverse_leadingBlock` pad a local left inverse of the leading `(k+1) x (k+1)` block by zeros to produce the ambient leading-column left-inverse witness; `qrLeadingColumnLeftInverse` and `qrColumnNotInPreviousSpan_of_leadingColumnLeftInverse` then derive current-column independence from that witness. `exists_active_trailing_entry_ne_of_leading_witnesses` and `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_leading_witnesses` compose those two witness bridges to force a nonzero active trailing entry and positive trailing norm; the stored-loop wrapper `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_leading_witnesses_sqrt_budget` feeds the concrete witnesses, sign choice, and a square-root trailing-norm budget into the final nonzero-diagonal theorem. The local-inverse variants compose the two local `IsLeftInverse` block witnesses directly into the same nonbreakdown route. The determinant/rank variants use `exists_isLeftInverse_of_det_ne_zero` from `MatrixAlgebra.lean` to generate those local witnesses from nonzero determinants of the previous and current leading blocks, so the remaining route is proving those determinant/rank facts and usable budget lower bounds from a concrete full-rank/nonbreakdown/conditioning invariant for the computed loop. |

`fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_leadingBlock_offdiag_budget_of_signed_stage_budgets_factor`,
