`LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_normSqBudget_stageBudget_rowBudget_product`, `scalarAffineGrowthBudget`, `scalar_affine_growth_iterate_bound`, `coxHigham_rowSorting_active_entry_bound_of_stage_growth_with_additive`, `coxHigham_rowwise_error_accumulation_bound`, `coxHigham_abs_entry_le_exact_bound_add_error`, `coxHigham_rowSorting_active_entry_bound_with_accumulated_error` | Source-backed dependencies for the pivoted/sorted weighted least-squares QR route. The denominator theorems prove the Cox--Higham Lemma 2.1 lower bound `2||x_tail||_2^2 <= v^T v` under the standard sign convention, and `householderBeta_mul_inner_self_eq_two` exposes the exact beta normalization needed for orthogonality. The column-pivoting theorems prove that a finite remaining active-column set has a maximal trailing-norm pivot and that this pivot-max property gives the Cauchy--Schwarz comparison `|v^T y_tail| <= ||v||_2 ||x_tail||_2`. The scalar endpoint combines these facts into `|beta * v^T y_tail| <= sqrt 2`, and the row-growth theorem records the equation (4.3) entry estimate `|a_ij - phi*a_ik| <= (1 + sqrt 2) B` under a row-entry bound `B`. The exact same-reflector bridge rewrites the Householder matrix-vector update as that scalar Cox--Higham row update for non-pivot active rows, and then applies the signed column-pivot row-growth theorem directly. The row-sorting theorems formalize the stage accumulation from repeated `(1 + sqrt 2)` growth and sorted initial row bounds. The pivot-row active-tail theorem packages the equation-(4.4) norm step with an explicit ambient `sqrt m` factor, while the exact signed-pivot pivot-row bridge proves that the Householder matrix-vector pivot entry is bounded by the active-tail norm and composes this with the row-sorted stage budget. The active-row case-split theorem combines the non-pivot and pivot-row one-step bridges with the honest unified factor `max (1 + sqrt 2) (sqrt m)`, and `one_le_coxHighamActiveRowGrowthFactor` lets prefix rows inherit the same factor after exact zero-prefix preservation. The active-block wrapper proves that one uniform active-block bound supplies the separate row and column stage fields for a signed-pivot step, and the active-block sequence wrappers replace the separate row/column stage families by one active-block family at every stage. The initial-block propagation theorem proves that active-block family from an initial entrywise bound under monotone active windows plus the visible positive-norm and pivot-max fields. The exact signed-pivot panel sequence theorems propagate visible Cox--Higham stage budgets through a concrete exact loop object, including a geometric `c^t B0` wrapper. The factor-parametric and stage-budget stored-panel recurrences transfer exact same-reflector bounds into the rounded compact-update budget; the signed-pivot active-block sequence theorem packages the concrete vector/beta fields and propagates rounded active-block budgets `B_t` under visible nonbreakdown, pivot-max, storage, and compact-budget recurrence assumptions. The signed-stage prefix/active theorem `storedQRSignedStage_exact_same_reflector_bound_of_prefix_or_active_stage_bounds` packages the local exact same-reflector split for the actual stored QR signed trailing stage: prefix rows are preserved by the zero-prefix Householder identity, while active rows use the Cox--Higham signed-pivot bound. The displayed leading-block bridge `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_leadingBlock_offdiag_budget_of_exact_stage_budgets_factor` converts those stage budgets into the raw upper off-diagonal `rowBudget` field, using `qrLeadingOffdiagStop` and the completed-column preservation lemma to distinguish current and already completed columns. Its signed-stage companion specializes the bridge to the actual stored QR signed trailing Householder stages, removing the generic reflector-family parameter while keeping the stage-budget, pivot-zeroing, exact same-reflector, and terminal domination fields visible. The source-control handoff theorems compose that bridge with the row-budget decomposition so QR solve certificates can consume stage budgets and diagonal lower bounds directly. The additive perturbation theorems formalize scalar row-entry error accumulation and the exact-plus-computed row bound. This is not the final row-wise QR theorem: diagonal lower bounds, raw-stage nonbreakdown/rank, and the concrete pivoted/sorted policy fields remain open. |
| Cox--Higham active-norm nonbreakdown bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | active trailing column norms; column-pivot maximality; exact signed-pivot active-block sequence | `householderTrailingColumnNorm2Sq_pos_of_pivot_max_exists_pos`, `householderTrailingColumnNorm2Sq_pos_of_pivot_max_exists_active_entry_ne`, `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanel_sequence_active_block_bound_of_initial_block_bound_of_active_block_nonzero` | Reduces the exact signed-pivot sequence's direct positive active trailing-norm hypothesis. A remaining active column with positive norm, or more concretely a nonzero entry in the remaining active block, combined with pivot maximality forces the chosen pivot column to have positive active norm. The sequence wrapper then reuses the monotone-window active-block propagation theorem with a source-shaped nonzero-active-block field. This closes only the positive-norm reduction; the concrete pivot-max/nonzero-active-block fields and final QR/preconditioner theorem remain open. |
| Cox--Higham active max-pivot selector | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | finite remaining-column max selection; exact signed-pivot active-block sequence | `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn`, `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn_ge`, `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn_pivot_max`, `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanel_sequence_active_block_bound_of_initial_block_bound_of_active_max_pivot` | Supplies the pivot-max field by construction from the local finite active-column maximum theorem. The sequence wrapper replaces the raw pivot-max inequality by the source-shaped policy equation that the current pivot column is `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn` for the current active block. This closes only the pivot-max reduction for the current exact sequence shape; it still keeps the active-nonzero/nonbreakdown field visible and does not close the rounded stored-loop or QR/preconditioner theorem. |
| Cox--Higham active-block mass bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | active trailing block squared mass; finite active max-pivot policy; exact signed-pivot active-block sequence | `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq`, `exists_active_entry_ne_of_householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos`, `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanel_sequence_active_block_bound_of_initial_block_bound_of_active_block_norm_pos` | Replaces the existential active-nonzero field by a scalar positive active-block mass condition. `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq` sums squared entries over active rows and remaining columns; positivity gives an explicit nonzero active entry, which feeds the nonzero-active-block wrapper and the active max-pivot sequence theorem. This is still a scoped Cox--Higham dependency: a concrete pivoted/sorted loop must supply positive active-block mass and the active max-pivot policy before the final QR/preconditioner theorem can close. |
| Cox--Higham swapped active max-pivot policy | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | column swap; finite active max column; displayed post-swap pivot-max field; exact signed-pivot active-block sequence | `householderSwapColumns`, `householderTrailingColumnNorm2Sq_swapColumns_left`, `householderTrailingColumnNorm2Sq_swapColumns_of_ne`, `householderSwapColumns_activeMaxPivotColumn_pivot_max`, `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanel_sequence_active_block_bound_of_initial_block_bound_of_swapped_active_max_pivot` | Formalizes the concrete one-step sorting policy for the Cox--Higham route. After selecting the finite active max column and swapping it into the displayed active position, the post-swap active column satisfies the pivot-max inequality over the active suffix. The sequence wrapper combines this post-swap policy with positive active-block mass to obtain the exact active-block budget family. This closes the active max-pivot policy field for displayed swapped stages, but still leaves positive active-block mass/nonbreakdown, rounded stored-loop assembly, and the final QR/preconditioner theorem open. |
| Cox--Higham raw-to-swapped active-block mass bridge | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | active-block squared mass; nonzero active entry; column swap in active suffix; exact swapped-stage active-block sequence | `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_of_exists_active_entry_ne`, `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_swapColumns_pos_of_pos`, `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanel_sequence_active_block_bound_of_initial_block_bound_of_swapped_active_max_pivot_of_raw_active_block_norm_pos` | Pushes the active-block nonbreakdown condition through the concrete swap. A nonzero active entry implies positive active-block squared mass, and swapping a column inside the active suffix preserves positivity of that mass. The exact sequence wrapper can therefore assume raw-stage active-block mass before the active max-column swap, rather than a separate post-swap positivity hypothesis. This still leaves raw-stage nonbreakdown/rank and the final rounded QR/preconditioner solve theorem open. |
| QR active-block nonbreakdown from rank/determinant data | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | prefix-span QR nonbreakdown; local leading-block left inverses; previous/current leading-block determinants; active-block squared mass | `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_of_column_notInPreviousSpan`, `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_of_leading_witnesses`, `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_of_leading_block_leftInverses`, `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_of_leading_block_det_ne_zero`, `householderActiveBlockNorm2Sq_pos_sequence_of_leading_block_det_ne_zero` | Converts the repository's existing QR nonbreakdown routes into the positive active-block mass field required by the Cox--Higham raw-stage theorem. The determinant stagewise wrapper says that, at each stored QR stage, nonsingular previous/current leading blocks plus the stored lower-zero shape imply positive mass in the raw active block. This closes the local rank-to-active-mass dependency; the final concrete QR/preconditioner theorem still has to supply the determinant/lower-zero fields for its chosen loop and assemble the solve certificate. |
| QR off-diagonal-control route eliminations | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | source-shaped leading blocks; active-block mass; active max-pivot policy; active-block budgets; row-wise off-diagonal domination; row-budget diagonal lower bounds; row-max scalar defect; stage-budget/row-max comparison | `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_implies_offdiag_le_diag`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_offdiagBudget_implies_rowBudget_diag`, `activeMaxPivotRowBudgetDiagCounterexampleA0`, `activeMaxPivotRowBudgetDiagCounterexampleSeq`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_offdiagBudget_implies_rowBudget_diag`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_rowBudget_diag`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_property_implies_rowBudget_diag`, `activeMaxPivotRowBudgetDiagCounterexample_rowMaxDiagDefectBudget_pos`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_rowMaxDiagDefectBudget_nonpos`, `not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_stageBudget_le_rowMax` | Records checked counterexamples for the rectangular QR bottleneck. Even upper-triangular nonsingular displayed leading blocks together with positive active-block mass do not imply the row-wise off-diagonal domination field required by `StoredQRSourceOffDiagonalControl`. The stronger row-budget split obstruction adds a valid upper off-diagonal row budget but shows the matching diagonal lower-bound field can still fail. The active max-pivot strengthening uses first-stage `[[2,1],[0,1]]` and second-stage `[[1,2],[0,1]]` to show the finite active max-pivot policy still does not imply the diagonal lower-bound field. The active-block-budget strengthening also assumes the same budget controls every active trailing-block entry at each displayed stage, and the diagonal lower-bound field still fails. The meta-property strengthening shows that appending an arbitrary auxiliary side property true of the witness cannot rescue this route either. The active-pivot row-max theorem shows the same active-pivot/budget surface does not imply nonpositive row-max scalar defect: at displayed stage one the row maximum is `2` and the displayed diagonal magnitude is `1`. The stage-budget/row-max comparison theorem uses the same active-pivot sequence with oversized budget `3`: active-block and displayed off-diagonal budget hypotheses hold, but the displayed row maximum at stage one is `2`, so `stageBudget <= rowMax` fails. Thus active-block nonbreakdown, active-block magnitude control, row-growth upper bounds, active-pivot selection, and unrelated side conditions cannot be used as hidden replacements for off-diagonal control, diagonal lower-bound nonbreakdown, row-max defect, or the row-max comparison; a final theorem must prove those fields from a stronger pivoting/ordering/growth invariant or keep them as visible source/domain assumptions. |

The older exact-sequence adapter keeps the exact Cox--Higham Lipschitz/sorting field explicit as `hexact`. The direct row-magnitude adapter is the cleaner source-shaped route when the exact same-reflector update of the current stored panel has the Cox--Higham row-growth bound. The stage-budget variants are closer to the signed-pivot source route: if the exact step supplies `c * B_t`, then the rounded stored step is bounded by `c * B_t` plus the concrete compact Householder component budget, and the sequence theorem propagates explicit budgets. The signed-pivot one-step wrapper composes these FP budgets with `coxHigham_exactSignedPivotPanelStep_active_entry_bound_of_stage_bounds`, while the stored active-block sequence theorem propagates rounded active-block budgets through the signed-pivot stored panel under visible positive-norm, pivot-max, completed-column, pivot-column, active-window monotonicity, and compact-budget assumptions. These theorems close floating-point per-step/stage-budget dependencies, not raw-stage nonbreakdown or the final QR/preconditioner theorem. |

`fintype_card_product_grid_index`, `rectOpNorm2Le_of_unit_ball_cover` | Large foundational file for exact matrix reasoning. `frobNorm` and `infNorm` are compatibility wrappers over Mathlib norms for legacy `RMatFn` APIs; new exact matrix-facing APIs should prefer `RMat`/`RSqMat`. The determinant bridge wraps Mathlib's nonsingular inverse as `nonsingInv` and turns a nonzero determinant into the local `IsLeftInverse` and `IsInverse` predicates, so algorithm files can expose rank/determinant assumptions without carrying raw inverse witnesses. The `opNorm2Le` and `rectOpNorm2Le` predicates state operator-2 bounds in vector-action form; rectangular products now compose at the vector-action level via `rectMatMulVec_rectMatMul` and `rectOpNorm2Le_rectMatMul`. The finite-type/dilation layer is the deterministic bridge for future square-matrix concentration theorems, and now includes identity/transpose, trace algebra, quadratic forms, PSD and Loewner-order predicates, Frobenius entry bounds, Frobenius-to-vector squared bounds, row-to-Frobenius squared norm control, vector `ℓ₂² <= ℓ₁²`, square Frobenius-to-infinity norm control `||A||_F^2 <= n||A||_∞^2`, a reusable two-sided Loewner transfer from exact bounds plus a Frobenius perturbation, quadratic-form finite-sum and PSD weighted-sum adapters, two-sided Loewner-to-absolute-quadratic-form control, direct operator-to-quadratic-form adapters, `M^2` positivity for symmetric matrices, `M^2 <= L^2 I` from an operator bound, the converse adapter from a squared Loewner bound to an operator event, a one-sided self-adjoint-dilation Loewner-to-rectangular-operator bridge, paired-vector dilation quadratic-form algebra, and a trace-of-square identity for self-adjoint dilations. The vector inner-product and squared-norm perturbation lemmas are reusable for turning operator, residual, and entrywise-budget bounds into quadratic-form/objective bounds, both on `Fin n` and arbitrary finite index types. The cover layer now also reduces an `n`-dimensional unit-ball cover to a one-dimensional interval grid, with radius loss `sqrt n` and product cardinality `|grid|^n`; this is cover geometry only, not a concentration theorem. |

These lemmas let variance proxies be summed, scaled, converted to PSD differences, pushed through trace monotonicity, and converted from scalar-identity Loewner/operator/operator-square bounds to explicit one-sided, two-sided, or dimensioned trace bounds without restating basis-vector calculations in algorithm files. |
| Perturbation theory | `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/PerturbationTheory.lean` | residual and perturbation quantities, Higham `κ∞` | `forward_error_from_residual`, `componentwise_forward_error`, `forward_error_from_backward_error`, `componentwise_forward_error_exact`, `normwise_forward_error_exact`, `infNorm_eq_sup_row_sum`, `kappaInf_eq_infNorm_mul_infNorm`, `infNorm_inv_le_of_kappaInf_le_and_norm_lower`, `infNorm_sq_budget_of_kappaInf_le_and_norm_lower`, `infNorm_inv_le_of_kappaInf_le_and_det_ne_zero`, `infNorm_sq_budget_of_kappaInf_le_and_det_ne_zero` | Converts residual/backward-error hypotheses into forward-error conclusions. The `κ∞` bridge proves that Higham's explicit row-sum condition number is the product of repository infinity norms and converts a condition-number bound plus a positive lower bound on `||A||∞` into an inverse-∞ squared budget for QR nonbreakdown routes. The determinant-facing variants use `infNorm_pos_of_det_ne_zero` to take the lower bound to be `||A||∞` itself. |

## Main Dependency Chains

The strongest internally derived results follow these chains:

```text
Model
  -> Rounding
  -> Summation
  -> DotProduct
  -> MatVec
  -> MatMul

FiniteProbability
  -> Markov / Chebyshev / Chernoff kernels
  -> real-valued exponential Markov kernel
  -> randomized high-probability algorithm analyses

Model
  -> Rounding and SubtractionFold
  -> ForwardSub / BackSub
  -> TriangularSolveCombined
  -> LU solve and Cholesky solve

Model
  -> DotProduct
  -> Norm2
  -> future Householder construction/application work

MatrixAlgebra + PerturbationTheory
  -> forward-error and residual-based analyses

MatVec + residual definitions
  -> IterativeRefinement
  -> StationaryIteration-style bounds

RandNLA element-wise sampling
  -> FiniteProbability
  -> rectangular Frobenius norm infrastructure
  -> rectangular vector-action operator-2 infrastructure
  -> squared-magnitude probabilities
  -> one-sample Algorithm 1 update stability
  -> deterministic trace hit-count reductions
  -> complete entrywise Algorithm 1 trace stability
  -> finite-probability Markov concentration for hitCount
  -> pairwise-Chebyshev concentration for hitCount
  -> canonical product-law Chernoff concentration for hitCount
  -> generic product-law scalar MGF factorization, exponential-Markov tails,
     finite-family union bounds, and support-aware pointwise MGF adapters for
     arbitrary one-step trace statistics
  -> finite-family self-adjoint-dilation quadratic-form MGF support,
     including support-aware variants for truncated laws
  -> high-probability stability with an explicit count budget Q
  -> canonical product-law two-coordinate independence
  -> residual-as-sum of one-sample mean-zero increments
  -> one-sample entrywise, Frobenius, and fixed-vector variance proxies
  -> nonconditional Frobenius residual second moment and Markov probability
  -> fixed-vector residual second moment and exact/FP fixed-vector Markov
     probability
  -> finite-test-set exact/FP Markov probability as a future net-argument
     support layer
  -> self-adjoint dilation of rectangular residuals
  -> finite matrix product/trace infrastructure for square concentration
  -> finite PSD/Loewner-order and quadratic-form infrastructure
  -> trace monotonicity and finite Loewner algebra for variance matrices
  -> trace-of-square identity for self-adjoint dilations
  -> quadratic-form variance proxy for squared dilation increments
  -> Loewner and PSD variance-matrix proxies for squared dilation increments
  -> trace variance proxies for squared dilation increments
  -> summed variance-parameter proxy over the s independent increments
  -> product-law expectation bridge for mean-zero dilation residuals and
     expected squared-increment variance matrices
  -> squared dilation Loewner event adapters to exact/FP spectral events
  -> dilation event transfers to exact/FP rectangular operator events
  -> source-aligned truncated elementwise-sampling transfer:
     hard-thresholding at eps/(2n) costs eps/2 in Frobenius/operator norm,
     and half-budget exact truncated residual events transfer to exact/FP
     residual events against the original matrix
  -> truncated-product-law support and bounded-increment prerequisites:
     positive-probability traces occur with probability one, truncation does
     not increase Frobenius norm, retained one-step residual increments have
     rectangular, self-adjoint-dilation operator, and squared-Loewner bounds,
     and these bounded-increment events, including the simultaneous
     Bernstein-boundedness event, hold with probability one under the truncated
     squared-magnitude law
  -> support-aware truncated finite-test dilation MGF theorem:
     positive-probability retained samples discharge the one-step
     quadratic-form bound for supplied test vectors
  -> generic unit-ball-cover geometry in MatrixAlgebra
  -> product-grid cover reduction from one-dimensional interval grids, with
     radius loss `sqrt n` and index cardinality `|grid|^n`
  -> covering-net support theorem: finite vector tests + Frobenius event
     imply exact/FP rectangular operator events under an explicit cover
  -> scalar entrywise Markov bounds and finite union-bound simultaneous
     entrywise residual event
  -> positive- and negative-trace-exponential eigenvalue tail interfaces for
     future matrix Bernstein/Khintchine proofs
  -> deterministic spectral transfer from exact equation (2)-style events
     to floating-point residual events
  -> deterministic Frobenius-event bridge for weaker exact residual events
     and their floating-point operator consequences
  -> deterministic entrywise-event bridge for weaker scalar union-bound
     residual events and their floating-point operator consequences

RandNLA row sampling
  -> FiniteProbability
  -> rectangular Frobenius norm infrastructure
  -> norm-squared row probabilities from equation (4)
  -> literal Algorithm 2 sampled rows scaled by 1 / sqrt(s*p_i)
  -> one-division floating-point stability
  -> product row-trace law
  -> probability-one support theorem for positive-probability sampled rows
  -> Gram matrices A^T A and Atilde^T Atilde
  -> elementwise unbiasedness of Atilde^T Atilde
  -> finite iid variance calculation from the product trace law
  -> equation (5) squared-moment and high-probability Frobenius bounds
  -> generic floating-point perturbation transfer lemma
  -> exact-sum rounded-sketch FP corollary with δτ = 0
  -> DotProduct.fl_dotProduct / dotProduct_error_bound
  -> fully floating-point Gram corollary with δτ = 0

RandNLA leverage-score row sampling
  -> MatrixAlgebra vecNorm2 / opNorm2Le
  -> RowSampling + RowSamplingGram equation (4)/(5) machinery
  -> orthonormal-column basis U
  -> equation (6) probabilities p_i = ||U_i*||_2^2 / n
  -> rowGram U = I and rowSqNormProbDen U = n
  -> one-sample rank-one PSD, expectation identity, and nI Loewner bound
     for the Oliveira/Tropp sharper subspace-embedding route
  -> row-sampling C*-trace-MGF product-law iteration and scalarization bridge
  -> equation (7) exact operator-2 event via Frobenius-to-operator transfer
  -> fully floating-point equation (7) corollary using rowSampleGramFullFpPerturbBudget

RandNLA random-projection preconditioning
  -> MatMul.fl_matMul and matMul_error_bound
  -> exact Algorithm 3 products PiL A, A PiR, and PiL A PiR
  -> MatrixAlgebra.IsOrthogonal and Frobenius invariance
  -> RowSamplingLeverage.HasOrthonormalColumns and rowSqNormProbDen_eq_nat_of_orthonormal_columns
     for deterministic leverage-basis and equation (6) denominator preservation
  -> MatrixAlgebra.diagMatrix and IsOrthogonal.diagMatrix_of_sq_eq_one
     for SRHT-style deterministic signed-diagonal preprocessing
  -> finite Rademacher moments, signed-linear-form MGF factorization, scalar
     Hoeffding tails, and HadamardFlat expected row-norm identity
  -> Markov/union and coordinate-Hoeffding all-row signed-Hadamard row-norm
     high-probability auxiliaries
  -> one-sided FP preprocessing bounds
  -> two-sided FP preprocessing bound by composing the first multiplication
     perturbation with the second multiplication bound

RandNLA least-squares sketching
  -> MatrixAlgebra rectangular matrix-vector products and vector norms
  -> vector inner-product and quadratic-form control from opNorm2Le
  -> residual objective lsObjective(A,b,x) = ||Ax-b||_2^2
  -> PreservesLSObjective, a deterministic 1 ± ε sketch-preservation predicate
  -> coordinate quadratic-error bridge from an operator event to PreservesLSObjective
  -> finite-probability transfer from any proved operator-event probability
  -> sketched minimizer residual-objective bound
     ||A x_hat-b||_2^2 <= ((1+ε)/(1-ε)) ||A x_ref-b||_2^2
```

When a target statement looks like a new algorithm-level stability result,
first identify whether it is:

- a local kernel result, such as summation, dot product, matvec, matmul, or a
  triangular solve;
- a composition result, such as LU solve, Cholesky solve, residual computation,
  or iterative refinement;
- a perturbation conversion, where an existing backward/residual bound needs to
  be converted into a forward-error bound.

## Library Health Notes

Last checked: 2026-05-25.

- The main dependency direction is coherent: `Model` and `Analysis` provide
  reusable infrastructure; algorithm files build on those foundations; higher
  algorithm modules compose lower kernel contracts.
- The current Lean tree has 72 `.lean` files, and the 2026-05-25 scan found no
  `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, `unsafe`, or `opaque` tokens in `LeanFpAnalysis`.
- Generic finite-probability material now lives in
  `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean`, not inside RandNLA. This keeps Markov,
  Chebyshev, and Chernoff kernels reusable for future randomized algorithms.
- RandNLA Algorithm 1 is organized across four algorithm modules:
  `ElementwiseSampling.lean` contains the deterministic update, trace, hit
  count, and stability-event vocabulary; `HitCountConcentration.lean` contains
  hit-count expectation, Markov/Chebyshev/Chernoff concentration, the canonical
  product trace law, and high-probability stability corollaries;
  `ElementwiseTraceMGF.lean` connects that product law to the C-star
  one-step Tropp trace-MGF theorem through complex and C-star marginal
  expectation adapters, last-sample conditioning, and the iid sampled-sum
  trace-MGF domination theorem; it also transfers that iid domination back to
  the finite-real `finiteTrace (finiteMatrixExp ...)` interface.  The
  `ElementwiseSpectral.lean` trace-MGF adapters instantiate this finite-real
  theorem with the actual Algorithm 1 self-adjoint dilation residual
  increments.  The square Drineas--Zouzias route now also has source-sharp
  vector-action, transpose-vector, quadratic-form, Loewner, C-star variance,
  and two-sided truncated trace-MGF tail skeletons at variance scale
  `n * ||Ahat||_F^2 / s^2`, source sample-complexity simplification,
  deterministic truncation transfer, and support-aware FP gamma-budget
  transfer.
  `ElementwiseSpectral.lean` adds the deterministic equation (2) transfer
  layer: if a future exact matrix-concentration theorem proves a rectangular
  `rectOpNorm2Le` residual event, the existing Algorithm 1 FP budget transfers
  it to the floating-point residual event. It also contains the current
  residual-increment variance-proxy layer: the one-step increment is mean-zero
  and has entrywise, Frobenius, and fixed-vector second-moment bounds, and the
  full residual has exact and FP fixed-vector and finite-test-set Markov
  support theorems.  For the literal untruncated squared-magnitude law, the
  file now also proves a nonconditional trace-MGF theorem with the exact
  input-dependent reciprocal-entry radius
  `elementwiseLiteralResidualSupportRadius s A`, and a scalar FP corollary
  whose additive rounded-sketch term is
  `sqrt(m*n) * gamma fp (s+1) *
  sum_{Aij != 0} ||A||_F^2 / |Aij|`.  This is an end-to-end literal-law bound
  with an expanded FP radius; it is intentionally not advertised as the
  source-uniform CACM rate. `MatrixAlgebra.lean` now also provides
  `rectUnitBallCover`, `realUnitIntervalCover`,
  `rectUnitBallCover_product_grid`,
  `fintype_card_product_grid_index`, and
  `rectOpNorm2Le_of_unit_ball_cover`; Algorithm 1 uses the cover-to-operator
  theorem to prove exact/FP covering-net support theorems from finite vector
  tests and a Frobenius residual event, while the product-grid theorem reduces
  a supplied one-dimensional interval grid to an `n`-dimensional cover with
  cardinality `|grid|^n`. The square finite layer now also has
  `finitePSD`, `finiteLoewnerLe`, symmetric-square PSD, `M^2 <= L^2 I` from
  an operator bound, and the Algorithm 1 dilation quadratic-form variance
  proxy summed over `s` increments. These are still prerequisites, not the
  CACM spectral tail theorem.
- RandNLA Algorithm 2 row sampling is split between `RowSampling.lean` and
  `RowSamplingGram.lean`, with the leverage-score specialization in
  `RowSamplingLeverage.lean`. It is deliberately not modeled as a hit-count
  accumulator: Algorithm 2 returns an `s × n` sampled matrix. The corrected
  probabilistic analysis studies `AᵀA - ÃᵀÃ`, proving unbiasedness,
  the squared-Frobenius second moment, the high-probability form of equation
  (5), the probability-one support theorem for positive-probability row traces,
  the generic FP perturbation transfer lemma, the exact-sum rounded-sketch FP
  corollary, and the fully floating-point Gram corollary that reuses the
  repository dot-product theorem. In both final corollaries `δτ = 0`.
  `RowSamplingLeverage.lean` then applies this existing machinery to an
  orthonormal-column matrix `U`, proving equation (6), `UᵀU = I`, and equation
  (7) in vector-action operator-2 form, including the fully floating-point
  perturbation budget. `RowSamplingTraceMGF.lean` and
  `RowSamplingLeverageMGF.lean` now provide the sharper route prerequisites:
  row-trace product-law trace-MGF iteration plus the centered leverage
  covariance one-sample Bernstein log-CGF instantiation.
- RandNLA Algorithm 3 now lives in `Preconditioning.lean`. It deliberately
  reuses `fl_matMul` and `matMul_error_bound` for floating-point analysis,
  uses the existing orthogonal Frobenius-norm theorems for exact
  rotation-preservation facts, and reuses the local leverage-score
  `HasOrthonormalColumns` and denominator theorem to show that square
  orthogonal preprocessing preserves the equation (6) basis invariant. The
  paper's distribution-specific random projection uniformization discussion is
  documented as open rather than smuggled into a conditional theorem; the local
  coordinate-Hoeffding row-norm route is advertised only as a weaker auxiliary,
  not as Tropp's source-sharp SRHT lemma.
- `LeastSquaresSketch.lean` closes the deterministic equation (8) algebraic
  implication from a `1 ± ε` residual-objective preserving sketch to a relative
  squared-objective LS guarantee, plus the Algorithm 2 leverage-score
  finite-Loewner Bennett sample-budget theorem for the augmented-span row
  sketch. It also models the literal rounded sampled-row `A,b` construction and
  proves its rowwise residual perturbation bound plus the deterministic
  objective perturbation lift. The high-probability composition for the literal
  rounded objective is closed for exact rounded minimizers, for additive-gap
  approximate rounded minimizers with the solver objective gap explicit in the
  budget, and for solvers equipped with a componentwise forward-error
  certificate to an exact rounded minimizer. What remains open is deriving
  such a forward-error certificate from a concrete downstream
  solver/preconditioner FP pipeline.
- `docs/RANDNLA_CACM_NOT_PROVED_LEDGER.md` tracks the remaining CACM survey
  accuracy theorems that are not yet internally proved: Algorithm 1 equation
  (2) untruncated/fully rectangular matrix concentration, generic matrix
  Bernstein/Khintchine, random-projection LS embeddings, implementation-backed
  rounded LS sketches and solver/preconditioner transfer, low-rank equation
  (9), and matrix completion equations (10)--(11).
- The canonical RandNLA Chernoff theorems do not assume the MGF bound. They use
  `sqMagTraceProbability_chernoff_mgf_bound`, which is proved from the product
  trace distribution. The generic `*_of_mgf_bound` lemmas remain as reusable
  probability bridges, not as the final Algorithm 1 API.
- `HitCountConcentration.lean` is still large. It is internally sectioned and
  builds on `FiniteProbability`, but if more randomized algorithms are added,
  a future split into `HitCountMoments`, `HitCountBudgets`, and
  `SqMagTraceProbability` would be a reasonable maintenance refactor.
- `MatrixAlgebra.lean`, `MatrixInversion.lean`, and `IterativeRefinement.lean`
  remain the largest modules. They compile and are logically placed, but future
  maintainability would improve by splitting broad exact-algebra and high-level
  interface material once there is sustained work in those areas.

`PsumCountRBMap.stepNative_preserves_positive` | definitions / theorems | `Algorithms/OrderingExamples.lean` | Ordered-set foundation for the Psum `O(n log n)` route: predecessor/successor certificates around `-acc` are enough to prove that choosing the closer neighbor globally minimizes `|acc + x|`, and the sorted-list search layer proves that concrete predecessor/successor searches plus endpoint cases return such a minimizer. The selector fails exactly on empty sorted active lists. The deletion adapter proves the selected value plus the erased remainder permutes back to the active list, the erased remainder stays sorted, and its length drops by one. `PsumMinOrderFrom` and `psumSortedNeighborOrderFromFuel_minTrace` package this into a complete sorted-list minimizer trace without committing to the scan selector's tie-breaking. `PsumSortedNeighborLogSearchTraceFrom.cost_le_budget` and `psumSortedNeighborOrderFromFuel_logSearchTrace` prove the same recursive `n log n` budget for any sorted-neighbor implementation whose search/delete step satisfies the per-step logarithmic budget. The concrete `PsumRBSet` red-black-set specialization proves ordinary-real comparator facts, including `.eq` as real equality, sorted ordered traversal, lower/upper-bound neighbor certificates, endpoint failure certificates, concrete selector minimality, red-black logarithmic depth against the existing step budget, and depth-budget preservation after erase. The counted-entry bridge expands `(value, count)` entries into the source list view and proves that decrementing one counted occurrence realizes exactly one list deletion by permutation, length drop, and preservation of positive remaining multiplicities, including duplicate active values; positive-count expansion is also proved to have length at least the distinct-entry list. The counted `PsumCountRBMap` layer now connects that view to a concrete red-black map with positive multiplicities: lower/upper-bound queries over map keys return Psum neighbor certificates for the expanded active list, endpoint failures exclude expanded active values on the corresponding side, the counted-map selector returns a global Psum minimizer, `eraseOne_entries_perm`/`eraseOne_entries_length` give the duplicate-preserving expanded-list deletion bridge for a selected key, `depth_succ_le_stepBudget` charges the concrete red-black depth against the expanded active-list logarithmic step budget, `stepEntries_certifies` packages a successful selector/decrement step with minimizer, deletion, length-drop, and cost certificates, the selector/step fail exactly when the expanded active-list view is empty, a successful step strictly decreases the expanded active-list length, and `stepEntries_preserves_positive_entries` carries the positive-multiplicity invariant to the next counted-entry list. The native-update substrate now connects expanded active members to concrete updates: `rbNode_append_toList` and `rbNodePath_del_toList` expose Batteries' deletion traversal, `PsumCountRBMap.find?_some_of_mem_toList` and `PsumCountRBMap.exists_find?_of_mem_activeList` recover positive native lookup counts, `PsumCountRBMap.find?_some_zoom`, `PsumCountRBMap.eraseOneNative_toList_of_find?`, and `PsumCountRBMap.eraseOneNative_toList_of_mem_activeList` lift the concrete `RBMap.alter` branch traversal from a local zoom to any expanded active member, `PsumCountRBMap.eraseOneNative_activeList_perm`/`PsumCountRBMap.eraseOneNative_activeList_length` prove the native next map realizes a one-occurrence expanded-list deletion, and `PsumCountRBMap.eraseOneNative_preserves_positive` plus `PsumCountRBMap.stepNative_preserves_positive` carry the native positive-count invariant. |

| `CorrectionFormulaTrace`, `CorrectionFormulaTrace.exact`, `correctionFormulaTrace`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_exact_sub_and_finite_error_add`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_sterbenz_and_finite_error_add`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_signed_sterbenz_and_finite_error_add`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_two_signed_sterbenz`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.finite_s_unconditional`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.of_error_obligations`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.finite_error_of_sameExponentScaledInteger`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.of_two_signed_sterbenz`, `correctionFormula_abs_order_not_imply_signed_sterbenz_exact_sum`, `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.of_exact_add`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_fastTwoSumFiniteCertificate`, `finiteCorrectionFormulaTrace_exact_of_exact_add`, `correctionFormulaAbstractCounterexample_not_exact` | structures / definitions / theorems | `Algorithms/CompensatedSum.lean` | Source-level rounded trace for Higham equation (4.7): `s = fl(a + b)` and `e = fl((a - s) + b)`. The finite round-to-even trace is closed conditionally: exact intermediate subtraction plus representable local error suffices; positive or signed Sterbenz discharges the subtraction; two signed Sterbenz certificates discharge both representability obligations and package them as a reusable `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate`; the rounded sum's finite field is automatic by `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.finite_s_unconditional`, and `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.of_error_obligations` shows that only `a-s` and `(a+b)-s` remain genuine certificate obligations. The helper `FastTwoSumFiniteCertificate.finite_error_of_sameExponentScaledInteger` closes the direct handoff from a shared signed scaled-integer lattice representation plus a `t`-digit coefficient gap to the certificate's true-error field. Exact first addition plus finite `b` also constructs the certificate and proves the exactness conclusion; any such certificate implies equation (4.7). `correctionFormula_abs_order_not_imply_signed_sterbenz_exact_sum` rules out deriving the first signed Sterbenz branch directly from the printed magnitude hypothesis alone. A concrete abstract-`FPModel` counterexample shows the relative-error model alone is insufficient. The remaining C4.4 gap is the full base-2 all-signs theorem deriving the two error-representability obligations from the printed `|a| > |b|` assumptions, with the exact-add split already closed. |

| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_gamma_rect`, `frobNormRect_sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_truncated_le_const_rect`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_gamma_rect`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_explicit_gamma_rect` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Rectangular source-budget FP gamma chain. The final displayed radius is `eps + sqrt(m*n)*(||Ahat||_F^2/tau)*gamma fp (s+1)`, and the source-only expansion is `eps + (2*m*n*||A||_F^2/eps)*gamma fp (s+1)`. The sampling law is exact; the rounded sampled updates are charged by the proved gamma budget. |
| `sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_truncated_le_const`, `frobNormRect_sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_truncated_le_const_square`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_gamma_square`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_explicit_gamma_square` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Readable scalar-radius expansion for the truncated Algorithm 1 source-variant FP theorem. The first two lemmas bound the internal entrywise budget matrix by the constant entry `(||Ahat||_F^2/tau)*gamma fp (s+1)` and its square Frobenius contribution by `n*(||Ahat||_F^2/tau)*gamma fp (s+1)`. The final corollary replaces this by the source-only term `(2*n^2*||A||_F^2/eps)*gamma fp (s+1)` for `tau=eps/(2n)`, so the truncated theorem no longer hides FP error inside a budget matrix. |
| `elementwiseTruncate_eq_self_of_forall_nonzero_entry_abs_ge`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_no_small_entries_square`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_no_small_entries_square`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_no_small_entries_rect`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_no_small_entries_rect` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Literal Algorithm 1 source-rate corollaries. In the square case, if every nonzero entry satisfies `eps/(2n) <= |A_ij|`, the source truncation is identity and the sharp square trace-MGF theorem yields the literal law `p_ij = A_ij^2/||A||_F^2` with sample budget `14*n*||A||_F^2*log(2(2n)/δ) <= s*eps^2` and FP radius `eps + (2*n^2*||A||_F^2/eps)*gamma fp (s+1)`. In the rectangular case, the source threshold is `eps/(2*sqrt(m*n))`, the sample condition is `4*(2*max(m,n)+(4*sqrt(2)/3)*sqrt(m*n))*||A||_F^2*log(2(m+n)/δ) <= s*eps^2`, and the FP radius is `eps + (2*m*n*||A||_F^2/eps)*gamma fp (s+1)`. Sampling probabilities are exact by convention; sampled-entry rescaling, accumulation, and residual arithmetic are charged by the displayed gamma term. |
| `elementwiseLiteralContributionRadius`, `elementwiseLiteralResidualSupportRadius`, `elementwiseLiteralContributionRadius_le_of_entry_abs_ge`, `elementwiseLiteralResidualSupportRadius_le_of_entry_abs_ge`, `smul_elementwiseLiteralContributionRadius_le_of_entry_abs_ge`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_literalTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_scaled_radius_supportRadius`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralEvent_literal_scaled_radius_gamma_supportRadius`, `sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_le_literalContributionRadius`, `frobNormRect_sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_le_literalContributionRadius`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_literal_scaled_radius_gamma_supportRadius`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_literalTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bennett_radius_supportRadius`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_literalTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bernstein_denominator_two_thirds_supportRadius`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_literal_ge_one_sub_delta_bernstein_denominator_gamma_supportRadius`, `algorithm1LiteralBernsteinDenominatorBudget_of_entry_floor`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_literalTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bernstein_denominator_entry_floor`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_literal_ge_one_sub_delta_bernstein_denominator_gamma_entry_floor` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Nonconditional literal Algorithm 1 trace-MGF/FP theorem under the exact squared-magnitude law. Writing `G(A)=sum_{Aij != 0} ||A||_F^2/|Aij|` and `H(A)=||A||_F+G(A)`, the exact support radius is `H(A)/s`, the variance scale is `max(m,n)*||A||_F^2/s^2`, and the scalar FP additive term is `sqrt(m*n)*gamma fp (s+1)*G(A)`. The exact displayed threshold at parameter `theta` is `log(2(m+n)/δ)/theta + max(m,n)*||A||_F^2*s/(theta*H(A)^2)*(exp(theta*H(A)/s)-theta*H(A)/s-1)`, plus that FP term. Thus, for fixed `theta` and small `theta*H(A)/s`, the order is `Theta(log((m+n)/δ)/theta + theta*max(m,n)*||A||_F^2/s + sqrt(m*n)*gamma fp (s+1)*G(A))`. The Bennett wrappers set the optimizer internally and prove the direct condition `log(2(m+n)/δ) <= r^2/(2W+(2/3)Lr)`, equivalently `s = Theta(((max(m,n)||A||_F^2+H(A)r) log((m+n)/δ))/r^2)`, with FP radius `r + sqrt(m*n)*gamma fp (s+1)*G(A)`. The new entry-floor wrappers prove `G(A) <= m*n*||A||_F^2/alpha` and `H(A) <= ||A||_F + m*n*||A||_F^2/alpha` when every nonzero entry has magnitude at least `alpha`; the exact floor budget has order `Theta(((max(m,n)||A||_F^2+(||A||_F+m*n*||A||_F^2/alpha)r) log((m+n)/δ))/r^2)`, and the rounded radius is `r + sqrt(m*n)*(m*n*||A||_F^2/alpha)*gamma fp (s+1)`. This theorem has no hidden budget matrix and charges sampled-update/residual arithmetic, but it exposes either reciprocal-entry or entry-floor dependence and is therefore not the source-uniform CACM equation-(2) rate. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_abs_rectSelfAdjointDilation_elementwiseTraceResidual_le_prod_abs_sub` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Scalar independent-copy symmetrization for each fixed coordinate of the exact Algorithm 1 self-adjoint-dilation residual. It combines the canonical squared-magnitude product-law mean-zero theorem with `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_abs_le_prod_expectationReal_abs_sub_of_expectation_eq_zero`, proving that the expected absolute residual coordinate is bounded by the expected absolute difference of two independent exact sketches. This is a source-uniform/Khintchine-route foundation only; it does not prove the final matrix Khintchine tail or CACM equation (2). |
| `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_vecNorm2_mean_le_expectationReal_vecNorm2`, `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_vecNorm2_sub_mean_le_prod_expectationReal_vecNorm2_sub`, `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_vecNorm2_le_prod_expectationReal_vecNorm2_sub_of_expectation_eq_zero`, `sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_vecNorm2_rectMatMulVec_elementwiseTraceResidual_le_prod_vecNorm2_sub` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Vector-valued Euclidean independent-copy symmetrization for finite probability spaces, plus the Algorithm 1 fixed-vector residual instantiation. For every fixed vector `x`, the expected Euclidean norm of the exact residual action `(A - Atilde) x` is bounded by the expected Euclidean norm of the difference of two independent exact residual actions. This is exact-law/exact-arithmetic Khintchine-route infrastructure only; it is not uniform over all vectors and does not prove the CACM equation (2) spectral-norm tail. |
| `FiniteProbability.rectOpNorm2Le_of_entrywise_mean_zero_of_copy_diff_rectOpNorm2Le`, `sqMagTraceProbability_rectOpNorm2Le_elementwiseTraceResidual_of_all_copy_diffs`, `algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent`, `algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_subset_exactSpectralEvent`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_ge_of_all_copy_diff` | theorems/definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Operator-predicate independent-copy bridge for Algorithm 1. If every exact independent-copy residual difference from a realized trace is rectangular-operator-bounded by `L`, then the realized exact residual itself is bounded by `L`; the event and probability wrapper transfer any proved probability bound for the all-copy-differences event to `algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent`. This uses the exact squared-magnitude law only to obtain entrywise mean zero and introduces no floating-point computation. It is a Khintchine-route bridge only; the probability tail for the copy-difference event remains the next source-uniform task. |
| `sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_le_const_of_entry_abs_ge`, `frobNormRect_sqMagTraceErrorBudget_zero_init_le_const_square_of_entry_abs_ge`, `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_frob_explicit_gamma_square` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Faithful nontruncated Algorithm 1 FP corollary for the literal squared-magnitude law `p_ij = A_ij^2/||A||_F^2`. It uses the weaker exact Frobenius/Markov high-probability event, intersects it with the probability-one positive-support event, and expands the local FP budget to the displayed radius `eps + n*(||A||_F^2/alpha)*gamma fp (s+1)` under the explicit nonzero-entry floor `alpha <= |A_ij|` for nonzero entries. It is not the sharp CACM equation (2) matrix-Bernstein theorem. |
| `fl_elementwiseSampleUpdate` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Floating-point one-sample update for RandNLA Algorithm 1. |
| `ElementwiseTrace` | abbreviation | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Deterministic sequence of sampled matrix entries for Algorithm 1. |
| `hitCount` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Number of trace steps hitting a fixed output entry. |
| `fl_elementwiseTraceSketch` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Entrywise floating-point trace update with no-ops on non-hit steps. |
| `sqMagTraceErrorBudget` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Deterministic stability budget using an upper bound `Q` for the hit counter. |
| `hitCount_le_steps`, `hitCount_eq_zero_of_forall_not_hit`, `fl_elementwiseTraceSketch_zero_init_eq_zero_of_forall_not_hit`, `sqMagTraceErrorBudget_nonneg` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Small local support lemmas used to derive FP budgets on the canonical sampler support instead of assuming a global all-traces perturbation hypothesis. |
| `FiniteProbability` | structure | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Lightweight real-valued finite probability space for randomized analyses. |
| `FiniteProbability.ext` | theorem | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Extensionality for finite probability spaces: two laws are equal when their mass functions agree at every finite outcome. Used to transport Algorithm 1 probability statements after proving a hard-thresholded input equals the literal input. |
| `FiniteProbability.eventProb_eq_one_of_forall` | theorem | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | If every finite outcome lies in an event, the event has probability one; used by the Rademacher signed-preprocessing event. |
| `FiniteProbability.boolUniformProbability`, `FiniteProbability.boolUniformProbability_prob`, `FiniteProbability.boolUniformProbability_expectationReal`, `FiniteProbability.entropyReal_boolUniformProbability_eq` | definition/theorems | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Unbiased Bernoulli coordinate probability law and exact formulas for its point masses, finite expectation, and entropy. This is a coordinate-measure dependency for the Ledoux product-measure route. |
| `FiniteProbability.twoPointEntropy_le_sq_sub_div_of_pos`, `FiniteProbability.entropyReal_boolUniformProbability_sq_le_sq_sub_of_pos` | theorems | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Scalar two-point entropy bound and positive-function Bernoulli coordinate log-Sobolev inequality `Ent(g^2) <= (g true - g false)^2`. This closes a real coordinate entropy inequality for the fair Bernoulli law; it does not yet tensorize to the full cube concentration theorem or Tropp Proposition 2.1. |
| `FiniteProbability.entropyReal_prod_boolUniformProbability_sq_le_coordinate_add_entropy` | theorem | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | One-coordinate product peel-off for the Ledoux tensorization route: `Ent_{P x Bool}(g^2)` is bounded by the expected Bernoulli-coordinate squared difference plus `Ent_P(E_Bool g^2)`. This closes the first product step after the coordinate log-Sobolev inequality; it still leaves the induction/L2-section norm step needed for the full Bernoulli cube theorem. |
| `FiniteProbability.prod_expectationReal_eq`, `FiniteProbability.prod_expectationReal_fst_eq`, `FiniteProbability.prod_expectationReal_snd_eq`, `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_abs_sub_mean_le_prod_expectationReal_abs_sub`, `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_abs_le_prod_expectationReal_abs_sub_of_expectation_eq_zero`, `FiniteProbability.entropyReal_prod_eq_expectation_entropyReal_add_entropyReal_expectation` | theorems | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Product-law Fubini for finite expectations, scalar independent-copy symmetrization around the mean, the centered scalar symmetrization corollary, and the exact entropy chain rule/tensorization algebra. This is a closed product-measure dependency for Ledoux/Tropp and Khintchine-style routes; it does not prove the coordinate log-Sobolev inequality or the Algorithm 1 matrix Khintchine tail. |
| `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_exp_pos`, `FiniteProbability.hasDerivAt_expectationReal_exp_mul`, `FiniteProbability.hasDerivAt_log_expectationReal_exp_mul`, `FiniteProbability.entropyReal`, `FiniteProbability.entropyReal_exp_mul_eq`, `FiniteProbability.log_mgf_differential_le_of_entropyReal_exp_mul_le`, `FiniteProbability.log_mgf_div_sub_quadratic_antitoneOn_of_differential_le`, `FiniteProbability.tendsto_log_mgf_div_nhdsGT_zero`, `FiniteProbability.log_mgf_le_mean_add_quadratic_of_differential_le`, `FiniteProbability.log_mgf_le_mean_add_quadratic_of_entropyReal_exp_mul_le`, `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_exp_centered_le_exp_of_log_mgf_le`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_real_le_mean_add_ge_one_sub_exp_sq_of_log_mgf_bound`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_real_le_mean_add_ge_one_sub_exp_sq_of_entropyReal_exp_mul_le` | definitions/theorems | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Finite MGF, entropy, and Laplace calculus for the Ledoux/Herbst route: positivity of finite exponential moments, derivative and log-derivative of finite exponential moments, the elementary real entropy functional, the entropy identity for exponential tilts, the entropy-bound-to-differential-inequality step, the corrected-quotient monotonicity step `λ ↦ log M(λ)/λ - cλ`, the right-limit `log M(λ)/λ -> E X` as `λ -> 0+`, the finite Herbst extraction `log M(λ) <= λ E X + c λ^2`, the entropy-bound-to-log-Laplace wrapper, the log-Laplace-to-centered-MGF step, and Chernoff tail wrappers from visible log-Laplace or entropy hypotheses. This is a closed algebraic and calculus substrate for proving a future log-Sobolev-to-Laplace theorem once the entropy inequality is formalized; it is not itself the Ledoux/Talagrand concentration inequality. |
| `hitCountAtMostEvent` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Random-trace event that an entry is hit at most `Q` times. |
| `sqMagTraceStabilityEvent` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSampling.lean` | Random-trace event that the stability bound with budget `Q` holds. |

| `sqMagChernoffOptimizedHitCountTail` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/HitCountConcentration.lean` | Optimized Chernoff upper-tail expression with `lam = log((Q+1)/(steps*p_ij))`. |

| `opNorm2Le` | predicate | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Vector-action form of `||M||₂ ≤ c`: for all `x`, `||Mx||₂ ≤ c ||x||₂`. |

| `matMulRectLeft` | definition | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Left multiplication of an `m × n` rectangular matrix by an `m × m` square matrix. |
| `matMulRectRight` | definition | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Right multiplication of an `m × n` rectangular matrix by an `n × n` square matrix. |

| `algorithm1ExactEntrywiseEvent` | event predicate | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact Algorithm 1 simultaneous scalar entrywise residual event used by union-bound support theorems. |
| `elementwiseTraceResidual` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact Algorithm 1 residual `A - Atilde` for a deterministic trace. |
| `fl_elementwiseTraceResidual` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Floating-point Algorithm 1 residual `A - fl(Atilde)` for a deterministic trace. |

| `rowSqNormProbDen_preconditionColumns_eq_nat_of_orthogonal` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | The equation (6) leverage denominator remains `n` after square right-orthogonal preprocessing of an orthonormal-column basis. |

| `IsOrthogonal.diagMatrix_of_sq_eq_one` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | A diagonal matrix whose entries have square one is orthogonal; this is the deterministic sign-diagonal fact for SRHT-style preprocessing. |
| `signedOrthogonalPreconditioner_isOrthogonal` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | If `H` is orthogonal and `sign_i^2 = 1`, then `H * diag(sign)` is orthogonal. |
