| `RademacherTrace` | abbreviation | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Finite Boolean sign trace used by the SRHT-style Algorithm 3 route. |
| `rademacherSign`, `rademacherSignVector` | definitions | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Maps Boolean sign traces to real `±1` diagonal entries. |
| `rademacherTraceFlip` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Boolean coordinate flip on a finite Rademacher trace; this is the coordinate move in the finite-cube entropy-gradient theorem. |
| `rademacherSignVector_flip_self`, `rademacherSignVector_flip_of_ne`, `rademacherSignVector_sub_flip` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Exact sign-vector algebra for one Boolean coordinate flip: the flipped coordinate changes by a factor of `2`, while all other coordinates are unchanged. These are used to compute signed-Hadamard row differences. |
| `rademacherTraceFlip_involutive`, `rademacherTraceFlipEquiv` | theorems/definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Coordinate flips are involutions and therefore finite-cube equivalences; this is the measure-preservation basis for tilt symmetrization. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Uniform finite probability law on sign traces. |
| `rademacherTraceProbMass_flip`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_flip` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Uniform Rademacher mass and finite expectations are invariant under a coordinate flip. |
| `rademacherTraceProbMass_snoc` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | The uniform Rademacher trace mass factors across the `Fin.snoc` last-coordinate split. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_succ_last_eq` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Conditions a real expectation under `rademacherTraceProbability (m+1)` on the last Boolean coordinate. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_succ_eq_prod` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Transports expectations between `RademacherTrace (m+1)` and the explicit product `RademacherTrace m × Bool`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_entropyReal_succ_eq_prod` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Transports finite entropy across the last-coordinate product split. |
| `rademacherTraceFlip_castSucc_snoc`, `rademacherTraceFlip_last_snoc` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Compatibility of coordinate flips with the `Fin.snoc` last-coordinate split. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_entropyReal_sq_le_sum_flip` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Concrete finite Bernoulli-cube entropy-gradient inequality for positive functions on `RademacherTrace m`, obtained by iterating the abstract Bernoulli-product induction lift over Boolean coordinate flips. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_le_of_pointwise_pair_le`, `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound_of_pointwise_pair_le` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Finite-cube symmetrization: pointwise pair bounds for exponential-tilt flip increments imply the summed expectation flip-gradient bound, using only flip invariance of the uniform Rademacher law. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound_of_pointwise_halfdiff_sq_le`, `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound_of_pointwise_absdiff_le` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Converts scalar half-tilt and coordinatewise absolute-difference bounds into the conditional entropy-gradient hypothesis. These are reusable non-sharp bridges; the source-sharp signed-Hadamard row-norm branch is closed separately by the positive-drop self-bounding theorems below. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_entropyReal_exp_mul_le_of_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Applies the concrete cube entropy-gradient inequality to the exponential tilt `exp (lam * X / 2)`, reducing the visible entropy hypothesis for Herbst to a deterministic flip-gradient bound on that tilt. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_real_le_mean_add_ge_one_sub_exp_sq_of_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Conditional finite-cube upper-tail theorem obtained by composing the exponential-tilt reduction with the local Herbst/Chernoff adapters; the remaining hypothesis is the deterministic flip-gradient estimate, not a concentration assumption. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_eq_zero` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Each finite Rademacher sign coordinate has expectation zero. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_mul_eq_ite` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Finite Rademacher signs have Kronecker-delta second moment. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sq_sum_mul_sign_eq_sum_sq` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Exact second moment of a finite Rademacher signed linear form. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_exp_sum_mul_sign_eq_prod` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Exact exponential-moment factorization for a finite Rademacher signed linear form. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_sum_mul_sign_le_ge_one_sub_exp_mul_prod` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | One-sided exponential-Markov tail skeleton for a signed linear form using the exact MGF factorization; this is a scalar concentration foundation, not Tropp's row-norm lemma. |
| `real_rademacher_cosh_factor_le_exp_sq_div_two` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Repository wrapper around the scalar Hoeffding/cosh inequality in the Rademacher normalization. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_exp_sum_mul_sign_le_exp_sum_sq_div_two` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Scalar Hoeffding MGF bound for a finite Rademacher signed linear form. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_exp_lam_sum_mul_sign_le_exp_lam_sq_sum_sq_div_two` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Parameterized scalar Hoeffding MGF bound with explicit exponential parameter `lam`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_sum_mul_sign_ge_le_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | One-sided upper tail for a finite Rademacher signed linear form from the local MGF bound and finite exponential Markov. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_abs_sum_mul_sign_le_ge_one_sub_two_mul_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Two-sided absolute-value Hoeffding tail for a finite Rademacher signed linear form. |
| `finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_matrix_sum_eq_sum` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Deterministically rewrites a fixed quadratic form of an exact Rademacher signed matrix series as a scalar signed linear form. |
| `finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_matrix_sum_eq_sum_fintype` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Type-polymorphic finite-index version of the signed-matrix quadratic-form identity, used for self-adjoint dilation indices such as `Fin m ⊕ Fin n`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_signed_matrix_sum_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Simultaneous finite-family two-sided Hoeffding bound for exact quadratic forms of an exact Rademacher signed matrix series. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_signed_matrix_sum_fintype_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Type-polymorphic finite-index simultaneous Hoeffding bound for exact quadratic forms of an exact Rademacher signed matrix series. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_diff_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Algorithm 1 instantiation of the finite-index Rademacher quadratic-form tail for fixed stepwise independent-copy residual-increment differences in self-adjoint dilation form. |
| `finiteUnitBallCover` | definition | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Generic finite-index Euclidean unit-ball cover predicate used for self-adjoint dilation spaces such as `Fin m ⊕ Fin n`. |
| `finiteLoewnerLe_of_finite_unit_ball_cover_quadraticForm` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Deterministic exact cover upgrade: finite quadratic-form tests at threshold `eta` plus a coarse exact operator radius `L` imply the Loewner upper bound with explicit radius `eta + L * (2 * rho + rho ^ 2)`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_rectOpNorm2Le_signed_sampleResidualIncrement_diff_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div_of_finiteUnitBallCover` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact Rademacher finite-cover operator event for fixed Algorithm 1 copy-difference increments. The theorem combines the finite-test Hoeffding event, `finiteUnitBallCover`, and a visible deterministic coarse signed-dilation operator radius to get a rectangular operator event for the signed copy-difference sum. It is intermediate infrastructure, not a final source-uniform spectral tail. |
| `algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_trace_residual_small_unit_diff_not_rectOpNorm2Le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Deterministic exact obstruction: for the literal one-step small-entry input `[1, (|L|+2)^{-1}]`, the residual difference between the tiny-entry trace and the unit-entry trace is not bounded by the proposed rectangular operator radius `L`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_ge` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact-law lower bound showing that the failure probability of the literal all-copy-differences event is at least the exact tiny-entry sampling probability for the one-step small-entry input. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_pos` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Positive-probability corollary of the exact lower bound for failure of the literal all-copy-differences event. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_delta_ge` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Quantitative necessary condition: any claimed `1 - delta` lower bound for the literal all-copy-differences event on the small-entry input forces `delta` to be at least the exact tiny-entry sampling probability. |
| `sqMagTraceProbMass_algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_all_tiny` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact product-law mass formula: the trace that samples the tiny entry at every one of `s` Algorithm 1 steps has mass `p_tiny ^ s`. |
| `algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_all_tiny_trace_residual_not_rectOpNorm2Le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Deterministic exact obstruction: for any positive sample count `s`, the all-tiny trace of `[1, (|L|+2)^{-1}]` is not in the exact spectral event at radius `L`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_ge` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact-law lower bound showing that the failure probability of the literal exact spectral event is at least the all-tiny trace mass `p_tiny ^ s`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_delta_ge` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Quantitative necessary condition: any claimed `1 - delta` lower bound for the literal exact spectral event on the sample-count small-entry family forces `delta >= p_tiny ^ s`. |
| `sqMagProb_algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_small_lt_one` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Denominator-positivity support for the divided obstruction: the tiny-entry sampling probability in the small-entry family is strictly less than one. |
| `log_inv_delta_le_nat_mul_log_inv_of_pow_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Generic logarithmic consequence of a power-mass obstruction: from `p ^ s <= delta` with positive `p` and `delta`, derive `log(1/delta) <= s * log(1/p)`. |
| `log_inv_delta_div_log_inv_le_nat_of_pow_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Generic divided logarithmic consequence: if `0 < p < 1`, `0 < delta`, and `p ^ s <= delta`, then `log(1/delta)/log(1/p) <= s`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_log_delta_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Algorithm 1 all-tiny obstruction in logarithmic sample-count form: any `1 - delta` lower bound for the small-entry exact spectral event forces `log(1/delta) <= s log(1/p_tiny)`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_sample_count_ge` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Algorithm 1 all-tiny obstruction in divided sample-count form: any `1 - delta` exact spectral-event lower bound forces `log(1/delta)/log(1/p_tiny) <= s`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_of_delta_lt_pow` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Direct impossibility wrapper: if `delta < p_tiny ^ s`, then the claimed `1 - delta` lower bound for the small-entry exact spectral event cannot hold. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_of_sample_count_lt` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Divided-log impossibility wrapper: for positive `delta`, if `s < log(1/delta)/log(1/p_tiny)`, then the claimed `1 - delta` lower bound for the small-entry exact spectral event cannot hold. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_le_one_sub_pow` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact success-probability cap: on the small-entry family, the radius-`L` exact spectral event has probability at most `1 - p_tiny ^ s`. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_lt_one` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Strict success-probability obstruction: for every positive sample count, the small-entry exact spectral event has probability strictly below one. |
| `exists_delta_not_sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Fixed-sample impossibility: for every positive sample count and radius `L`, there exists a positive `delta` for which the claimed `1 - delta` lower bound for the small-entry exact spectral event cannot hold. |
| `real_log_180000_le_18` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact numerical logarithm certificate used by the concrete source-budget witness: `log 180000 <= 18`, proved from `2 <= exp 1` and integer powers rather than a decimal approximation. |
| `algorithm1RectSourceBudget_1_2_100_one_div_30000` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | The fixed-parameter rectangular source-style budget predicate used by the concrete schema refutation: `m=1`, `n=2`, `s=1`, radius `100`, and `delta=1/30000`. |
| `algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_100_small_prob_gt_one_div_30000` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Concrete small-entry probability witness: for `A=[1,1/102]`, the tiny-entry probability is larger than `1/30000`. |
| `algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_100_rect_source_budget_one_div_30000` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Exact arithmetic source-budget witness: for `m=1`, `n=2`, `s=1`, `eps=100`, `delta=1/30000`, and `A=[1,1/102]`, the rectangular source-style budget holds. |
| `sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_rect_source_budget_witness` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Concrete incompatibility theorem: the same exact source-budget witness satisfies the source budget, but the exact literal squared-magnitude spectral-event lower bound at radius `100` is impossible. |
| `not_forall_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_of_rect_source_budget_one_div_30000` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseSpectral.lean` | Source-budget-only schema refutation: it is not true that every exact `1 x 2` input with positive squared-magnitude denominator satisfying the fixed rectangular source budget has exact spectral-event success probability at least `1 - 1/30000` at radius `100`. |
| `HadamardFlat` | predicate | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Scaled Hadamard-style flatness side condition `H i k ^ 2 = (m : ℝ)⁻¹`. |
| `signedHadamardPreconditionRows_entry` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Expands each signed-Hadamard preconditioned row entry as a Rademacher signed linear form. |
| `signedHadamard_entry_coeff_sum_sq_eq_inv` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Under `HadamardFlat` and `UᵀU=I`, the variance proxy for one signed-Hadamard entry equals `1 / m`. |
| `signedHadamard_row_inner_sq_sum_eq_inv_mul` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Parseval-style row identity: under `HadamardFlat` and `UᵀU=I`, summing squared row-support inner products over Hadamard coordinates gives `(m : ℝ)⁻¹ * ||u||₂²`. |
| `FiniteVecConvex` | predicate | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Repository-local convexity predicate for real-valued functions on finite vectors in the legacy `Fin n -> ℝ` representation. |
| `FiniteVecLipschitzWith` | predicate | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Repository-local Lipschitz predicate for real-valued functions on finite vectors, stated with the local Euclidean norm wrapper. |
| `unitCubeToRademacherVec` | definition | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Affine map `x ↦ 2*x - 1` from unit-cube coordinates to Rademacher-sign coordinates, used to track the factor-two constant conversion in the Ledoux/Tropp route. |
| `finiteVecConvex_scaled_unitCubeToRademacher` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Pulling a convex finite-vector function back along `unitCubeToRademacherVec` and scaling by a nonnegative constant preserves convexity. |
| `finiteVecLipschitzWith_scaled_unitCubeToRademacher` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | If `f` is `L`-Lipschitz and `0 < L`, then `(2*L)⁻¹ * f (2*x - 1)` is 1-Lipschitz. This formalizes the deterministic scaling that turns Ledoux's `[0,1]^n` exponent into Tropp's Rademacher exponent. |
| `vecNorm2_linear_combination_convex` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | The Euclidean norm of any finite linear map is convex, proved from the local homogeneity and triangle inequalities. |
| `signedHadamard_row_vecNorm2_convex` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Deterministic Tropp Proposition 2.1 input: the row norm `x ↦ ||(H diag x U)_{i,*}||₂` is convex in the sign vector. The Algorithm 3 SRHT row-norm theorem is proved through the row-specific positive-drop route below; the fully general Ledoux/Talagrand convex-Lipschitz proposition remains advisory. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_entry_abs_signedHadamard_le_ge_one_sub_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Coordinate Hoeffding bound for one signed-Hadamard preconditioned entry. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_entry_abs_signedHadamard_le_ge_one_sub_sum_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Finite union-bound coordinate Hoeffding event for all signed-Hadamard entries. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_rowNormSq_signedHadamard_le_ge_one_sub_sum_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Coordinate-Hoeffding all-row row-norm bound: all squared row norms are at most `n * B^2` with the displayed finite-entry failure budget. Weaker than Tropp's sharp SRHT row-norm lemma. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_leverageScoreProb_signedHadamard_le_ge_one_sub_sum_exp_sq_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Converts the coordinate-Hoeffding all-row row-norm event into the equation (6) leverage-probability event `leverageScoreProb <= B^2`, using signed-orthogonal denominator preservation. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_leverageScoreProb_signedHadamard_le_ge_one_sub_delta` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Delta-budget wrapper for the coordinate-Hoeffding leverage-probability event. This is a scoped weaker alternative, not Tropp's source-sharp SRHT theorem. |
| `uniformRowOuterGramSample` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | One-step uniform row outer-product estimator `m u_i u_iᵀ` used after Algorithm 3 preconditioning. |
| `finiteQuadraticForm_uniformRowOuterGramSample_eq` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Computes the quadratic form of the uniform estimator as `m * (u_i · x)^2`. |
| `finitePSD_uniformRowOuterGramSample` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Uniform one-step row outer-product estimators are PSD. |
| `uniform_rowOuterGramSample_mean_eq_id` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Under `HasOrthonormalColumns U`, the uniform one-step estimator has expectation `I`. |
| `uniformRowOuterGramSample_finiteLoewnerLe_of_leverageScoreProb_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | If `leverageScoreProb U i <= B^2`, then `m u_i u_iᵀ <= m n B^2 I` in finite Loewner order. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_uniformRowOuterGramSample_signedHadamard_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Composes the coordinate-Hoeffding leverage-probability event with the uniform one-step Loewner bound. This closes a dependency for the weaker uniform-after-preconditioning route, but not the concentration theorem for sample averages. |
| `uniformRowProb` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Uniform one-row probability mass used by the Algorithm 3 downstream uniform-sampling route. |
| `uniformRowTraceProbability` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Independent product law for iid uniform row traces after Algorithm 3 preprocessing. |
| `uniformRowSampleGram` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Uniform sample-average Gram estimator `s^{-1} sum_t m u_{i_t} u_{i_t}^T`. |
| `uniformRowSampleGram_sub_finiteIdMatrix_eq_centered_average` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSampling.lean` | Rewrites the centered uniform sample Gram as the average of centered one-step estimators. |
| `uniformRowOuterGramSample_centered_square_expectationCStarMatrix_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingMGF.lean` | Conservative one-step variance proxy for centered uniform row outer-product estimators under a deterministic row/Loewner bound. |
| `uniformRowTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_centered_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingMGF.lean` | Product-law trace-MGF upper-tail bound for iid centered uniform row outer-product samples. |
| `uniformRowTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_neg_centered_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingMGF.lean` | Matching lower-tail trace-MGF bound for the negated centered uniform samples. |
| `uniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_uniformRowSampleGram_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_of_tail_budget` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingMGF.lean` | Exact iid uniform sample-average concentration theorem in finite Loewner order, stated with explicit upper/lower trace-exponential tail budgets. |
| `uniformRowSampleSketch`, `fl_uniformRowSampleSketch` | definitions | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Exact and rounded uniform row sketches using scaling denominator `sqrt(s / m)` after Algorithm 3 preprocessing. |
| `rowSketchGram_uniformRowSampleSketch_eq_uniformRowSampleGram` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Identifies the exact uniformly rescaled sketch Gram with the sample-average Gram used by the exact concentration theorem. |
| `fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_perturb_bound` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Fully floating-point uniform sampled-Gram perturbation bound from local division stability and the row-sketch dot-product FP theorem. |
| `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | High-probability FP transfer for the scoped Algorithm 3 coordinate-Hoeffding route: the exact product-law two-sided Loewner event is preserved with radius enlarged by `uniformRowSampleGramFullFpPerturbBudget`. |
| `uniformRowSampleGramFullFpConstBudget` | definition | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Deterministic constant FP radius for the fully rounded uniform sampled Gram, parameterized by a scalar `C` bounding all sampled absolute-product sums. |
| `uniformRowSampleGramFpPerturbConstBudget_eq_nat_mul`, `uniformRowSampleGramDotProductConstBudget_eq_nat_mul` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Closed forms for the row-scaling and dot-product constant budgets, exposing the Gram dimension factor `n`. |
| `abs_mul_entry_le_rowNormSq`, `uniformRowSampleSketch_abs_mul_sum_le_of_rowNormSq_le` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Deterministic row-norm cap route: if every sampled row has squared norm at most `R`, then every uniform-sketch absolute-product sum is at most `m * R`. |
| `uniformRowSampleGramFullFpPerturbBudget_le_const_of_sample_rowNormSq_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Bounds the sample-dependent FP perturbation budget by `uniformRowSampleGramFullFpConstBudget fp s (m * R)` when all sampled rows obey `rowNormSq <= R`. |
| `signedHadamardFlUniformRowSampleGramTwoSidedConstBudgetEvent`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget` | definition/theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Deterministic-radius FP event and probability transfer: any scalar `τ` dominating the sample-dependent budget over the joint sign/sample space gives the same `1 - (δPre + δSample)` probability with radius `ε + τ`. |
| `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget_srht`, `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_uniformRowSampleGramDot_two_sided_finiteLoewnerLe_ge_one_sub_delta_constBudget_srht_log_preprocess` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/UniformRowSamplingFP.lean` | Source-sharp SRHT FP constant-budget transfer: on the same SRHT preprocessing event used in the exact product-law theorem, the sampled-row norm cap bounds the local FP perturbation by `uniformRowSampleGramFullFpConstBudget fp s (m * S^2)`, giving the rounded two-sided sample-Gram finite-Loewner event with probability at least `1 - (δPre + δSample)`, in explicit-`t` and logarithmic-preprocessing forms. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_rowNormSq_signedHadamard_eq` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Under finite Rademacher signs, `HadamardFlat H`, and `UᵀU = I`, each preconditioned row has expected squared norm `(n : ℝ) * (m : ℝ)⁻¹`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sqrt_rowNormSq_signedHadamard_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Expected Euclidean row norm is at most the square root of the expected squared row norm; this is used in the source-sharp SRHT row-norm tail below. |
| `hasOrthonormalColumns_vecNorm2Sq_mul_vec_eq`, `hasOrthonormalColumns_transpose_mul_vecNorm2Sq_le` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/RowSamplingLeverage.lean` | Deterministic orthonormal-column linear algebra: multiplying by `U` preserves coefficient-vector norm, and the transpose action is a contraction. These are reusable prerequisites for SRHT row-norm Lipschitz bounds. |
| `vecNorm2_inv_smul_self_of_pos`, `vecInnerProduct_inv_smul_self_eq_norm`, `vecNorm2_sub_le_inner_unit_diff` | theorems | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Unit-support-vector and norm-difference support lemmas: normalize a nonzero vector, compute its inner product with the original vector, and bound a Euclidean norm drop by an inner product against a unit support vector. |
| `abs_vecNorm2_sub_le_vecNorm2_sub` | theorem | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Reverse triangle inequality for the repository Euclidean vector norm, used to convert row-vector differences into row-norm Lipschitz bounds. |
| `signedHadamard_row_vecNorm2_lipschitz` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Deterministic Tropp Lemma 3.3 input: for flat `H` and `UᵀU=I`, the row norm `x ↦ ||(H diag x U)_{i,*}||₂` is `sqrt((m : ℝ)⁻¹)`-Lipschitz. |
| `signedHadamard_row_vec_sub_flip`, `signedHadamard_row_vecNorm2_positive_flip_sq_sum_le` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Exact coordinate-flip row-difference formula and sharp deterministic positive-flip self-bounding estimate for one signed-Hadamard row-norm function: \(\sum_k \max(F_i(\omega)-F_i(\omega^k),0)^2 \le 4/m\). |
| `real_exp_half_sub_sq_le_quarter_mul_sq_mul_exp_of_le`, `real_exp_half_sub_sq_le_lam_sq_quarter_pair_pos` | lemmas | `Analysis/FiniteProbability.lean` | Scalar ordered half-exponential inequality and its two-sided positive-drop version; these are the analytic bridge from positive coordinate drops to tilted flip-gradient bounds. |
| `real_sqrt_eight_log_div_pos_of_pos_lt`, `real_mul_exp_neg_mul_sqrt_eight_log_div_sq_div_eight_eq` | lemmas | `Analysis/MatrixConcentration.lean` | Scalar logarithmic preprocessing-budget algebra: for `0 < δ < B`, the radius `sqrt (8 * log (B / δ) / B)` is positive and makes `B * exp (-(B * t^2 / 8)) = δ`. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound_of_pointwise_posdiff_sq_sum_le` | theorem | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Finite-cube positive-drop tilt bridge: a pointwise bound on \(\sum_i \max(X(\omega)-X(\omega^i),0)^2\) yields the squared exponential-tilt flip-gradient bound consumed by the entropy/Herbst wrapper. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_flip_tilt_sq_sum_bound_signedHadamard_row_vecNorm2`, `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_vecNorm2_signedHadamard_le_mean_add_ge_one_sub_exp_m_t_sq_div_eight` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Source-sharp one-row SRHT concentration: the signed-Hadamard row-norm positive-drop estimate gives the exponential-tilt hypothesis and hence the tail \(1-\exp(-m t^2/8)\) for one row. |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_vecNorm2_signedHadamard_le_sqrt_add_ge_one_sub_m_exp_m_t_sq_div_eight`, `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_rowNormSq_signedHadamard_le_sqrt_add_sq_ge_one_sub_m_exp_m_t_sq_div_eight` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Tropp-style all-row SRHT row-norm flattening in explicit-`t` form: all rows satisfy \(||e_i^T H D U||_2 \le \sqrt{n/m}+t\), equivalently squared row norm bounded by \((\sqrt{n/m}+t)^2\), with failure budget \(m\exp(-m t^2/8)\). |
| `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_leverageScoreProb_signedHadamard_le_sqrt_add_sq_div_nat_ge_one_sub_m_exp_m_t_sq_div_eight`, `rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_leverageScoreProb_signedHadamard_le_sqrt_add_sq_div_nat_ge_one_sub_delta` | theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/Preconditioning.lean` | Converts the source-sharp all-row SRHT row-norm event into the equation-(6) leverage-probability cap \((\sqrt{n/m}+t)^2/n\), with either explicit failure budget \(m\exp(-m t^2/8)\) or a supplied delta budget. |

| `rectLSGram`, `rectLSRhs`, `RectLSNormalEquations`, `rectLSNormalEquations_perturbed_to_gram_system`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_rectangular_perturbed_normal_equations` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Rectangular least-squares normal-equation bridge for future QR/preconditioner work. | `lsQRSolveBackwardSolverDx`, `lsQRSolveBackwardSolverDx_nonneg`, `gram_forward_error_certificate_of_ls_qr_solve_backward_error` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/RandNLA/LeastSquaresSketch.lean` | Adapter from the local `LSQRSolveBackwardError` specification to the same componentwise `solverDx` certificate. It converts the spec's Frobenius Gram perturbation radius to entrywise control using `abs_entry_le_frobNorm` and uses `max c_g 0` for the right-hand-side radius, so no nonnegativity artifact is hidden. This remains a spec adapter, not a proof that a concrete QR implementation satisfies the spec. |
| `normalEqCholeskyXHat`, `normalEqCholeskySolverDx`, `normal_equations_cholesky_forward_error_certificate` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSNormalEquations.lean` | Concrete normal-equations/Cholesky solver certificate. It reuses `ls_normal_equations_backward` and `ls_normal_equations_forward_error` to produce the componentwise solver certificate consumed by the RandNLA literal rounded sampled-row equation (8) theorem. This closes a concrete solver route, but it is the normal-equations route with Gram conditioning, not a rectangular QR/preconditioner proof. |
| `rect_orthogonal_sequence_one_step` | theorem | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Rectangular one-step orthogonal-transformation accumulation theorem. It generalizes the square Householder accumulation step to `m × n` data using `matMulRectLeft` algebra and rectangular Frobenius bounds. This is a source-style one-step engine, not the full multi-step concrete rectangular QR/preconditioner theorem. |
| `rect_orthogonal_sequence_geometric` | theorem | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Rectangular multi-step orthogonal-transformation accumulation theorem. It iterates the one-step result over `r` perturbed orthogonal transformations and proves the rigorous geometric perturbation radius `((1+c)^r-1)||A||_F`. This is still an accumulation theorem over supplied transformations, not a concrete `fl_householder_qr` implementation or solve theorem. |
| `orthogonal_vector_sequence_one_step`, `orthogonal_vector_sequence_geometric` | theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Vector/right-hand-side companions to the rectangular accumulation theorems. They prove that the same supplied perturbed orthogonal transformations acting on `b` preserve a source-style `Q^T(b+Δb)` representation with rigorous Euclidean radius `((1+c)^r-1)||b||_2`. These are still supplied-transformation accumulation theorems, not a concrete QR/preconditioner solve implementation. |
| `rect_orthogonal_matrix_vector_sequence_one_step`, `rect_orthogonal_matrix_vector_sequence_geometric` | theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Simultaneous rectangular matrix/right-hand-side accumulation theorem. It applies the same supplied perturbed orthogonal transformations to `A` and `b`, producing one common orthogonal factor `Q` and perturbations `Delta A`, `Delta b` with geometric radii for both. This closes the common-`Q` substrate needed by the rectangular QR route; it still does not instantiate a concrete `fl_householder_qr` implementation or top-block shape theorem. |
| `RectLSNormalEquations.of_orthogonal_left` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Orthogonal row-transformation handoff for rectangular least squares. If `A_hat = U A` and `b_hat = U b` with `U` orthogonal, then exact normal equations for `(A_hat,b_hat)` imply exact normal equations for `(A,b)`. This is the deterministic solve-handoff substrate after a QR factorization theorem supplies transformed data. |
| `RectLSNormalEquations.of_rowwise_normal`, `RectLSNormalEquations.of_top_solve_zero_bottom` | theorems | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | QR solve-exactness substrate. A rowwise normal-equation identity implies the rectangular normal equations; in particular, if transformed data has top block `R`, zero lower matrix block, unrestricted lower transformed RHS, and the computed vector solves `R x = c`, then it satisfies the normal equations for the transformed rectangular problem. |
| `rectTopBlock`, `rectTopBlock_top`, `rectTopBlock_bottom`, `rectTopBlock_add`, `rectTopBlock_frobNorm_perturb_bound`, `rectTopBlock_frobNorm_perturb_bound_of_gamma`, `RectLSNormalEquations.exists_topBlock_of_fl_backSub`, `RectLSNormalEquations.exists_original_perturbation_of_commonQ_topBlock_fl_backSub`, `RectLSNormalEquations.exists_original_perturbation_of_commonQ_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_commonQ_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_rect_orthogonal_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_compact_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_pivot_error_lt_abs_alpha`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_pivot_ne_alpha_and_pivot_error_lt_abs_alpha`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_trailingNorm_pos_mul_nonpos_and_pivot_error_lt_abs_alpha`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_span_nonbreakdown_active_entry_budget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_leading_block_det_ne_zero_sqrt_budget` | definition/theorems | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Floating-point triangular-solve substrate for the rectangular QR route. `rectTopBlock` embeds an `n × n` top block into an `m × n` matrix with zero lower rows; the `fl_backSub` theorem produces a perturbation `Delta R` with `|Delta R_ij| <= gamma_n |R_ij|` such that the computed back-substitution result satisfies the rectangular normal equations for the perturbed top block. The common-`Q` pullback theorem then expresses this top-block perturbation as an original-data perturbation `Delta A + Q [Delta R;0]`; the strengthened version bounds the Frobenius radius by `||Delta A||_F + gamma_n ||[R;0]||_F`. The `LSQRSolveBackwardError` wrappers feed this result into the local QR solver specification for already-transformed common-`Q` data, a supplied sequence of perturbed orthogonal transformations, the compact Householder dot/scale/subtract sequence under explicit final-shape hypotheses, or the stored trailing Householder loop. The stored-loop handoff now reuses `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_higham_columnwise_factorization` for the common `Q`, columnwise perturbation radii, final `[R;0]`/top-RHS shape, and upper-triangularity. The pivot-error variant obtains the triangular-solve nonzero diagonal condition from the local per-pivot FP nonbreakdown inequality `budget_k < |alpha_k|`; the pivot-ne-alpha variant additionally replaces the denominator side condition by `A_hat[k,k] != alpha_k`; the sign-choice variant derives that scalar condition from positive trailing norm plus `alpha_k * A_hat[k,k] <= 0`; the prefix-span active-entry wrapper derives both the denominator and diagonal conditions from prefix-span nonbreakdown plus a concrete active-entry magnitude budget; and the nonsingular-leading-block variant derives positive trailing norm from nonzero local leading-block determinants plus lower-zero shape, while still keeping the square-root pivot budget visible. |
| `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_triangular_leading_blocks_sqrt_budget` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Solver-facing stored QR certificate for the triangular principal-minor route. It generates the previous/current leading-block determinant hypotheses from visible upper-triangular local shape and nonzero leading diagonal assumptions, then reuses the nonsingular-leading-block square-root-budget wrapper. |
| `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_span_nonbreakdown_active_entry_budget` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Solver-facing stored QR certificate for the prefix-span plus active-entry-budget route. It derives the Householder denominator and final nonzero stored diagonal from prefix-span nonbreakdown, sign choice, Householder normalization, and a visible active-entry magnitude budget, then reuses the stored-loop least-squares QR handoff. |
| `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget_of_span_nonbreakdown_trailingNorm2Sq_budget` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Solver-facing stored QR certificate for the prefix-span plus dimensioned trailing-norm route. The finite norm-margin bridge turns `m * budget_k^2 < ||A_k(k:m,k)||_2^2` into an active-entry witness, then the active-entry solver certificate supplies `LSQRSolveBackwardError`. This is a conditioning-ready domain theorem, not a proof that a conditioning invariant supplies the margin. |
| `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_prefix_lower_zero_pivot_nonzero_kappaInf_selfNorm_normSqBudget` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Explicit compact-budget prefix-local QR certificate with the pivot budget stated as a dimensioned trailing-norm-square margin. It keeps the repository-defined `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget` and final `qrSolveFinalGramBudget`/`qrSolveFinalRhsBudget`, replaces the visible square-root pivot-budget assumption by `m * budget_k^2 < ||A_k(k:m,k)||_2^2`, and derives the square-root form internally using `budget_lt_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_dim_mul_budget_sq_lt_trailingNorm2Sq`. It still leaves current-pivot nonzero, local `kappaInf`, and dual/conditioning-budget assumptions visible; use the following structured-leading-block wrappers when determinant nonzeroness of the displayed local leading blocks is the intended pivot route. |
| `qrPivotCounterexample2_first_pivot_zero`, `qrPivotCounterexample2_det_ne_zero`, `not_forall_det_ne_zero_implies_first_pivot_ne_zero` | definition/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Route-elimination facts for the rectangular QR bottleneck. The `2 × 2` column-swap matrix is nonsingular but has zero first unpivoted pivot, so generic determinant/full-rank information alone cannot close the current-pivot nonbreakdown hypothesis. Use these facts to prevent replacing the visible no-pivot condition by ordinary full rank without pivoting or a stronger structured invariant. |
| `diag_ne_zero_of_upper_triangular_det_ne_zero`, `qrLeadingBlock_current_pivot_ne_zero_of_local_upper_triangular_det_ne_zero`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_current_pivot_ne_zero_of_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero`, `qrPrefixSupportSpannedByPreviousColumns_of_leadingBlock_upper_det_ne_zero`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_prefix_lower_zero_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_normSqBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_prefix_lower_zero_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget`, `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_prefix_lower_zero_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_invNorm_dualBudget` | theorems | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean`, `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Structured current-pivot and norm-margin route for the rectangular QR bottleneck. The shared triangular determinant converse proves that an upper-triangular finite real matrix with nonzero determinant has nonzero diagonal entries. The QR wrappers use the stored lower-zero shape to turn nonzero determinant of each displayed local leading block into the current pivot nonzero condition and the prefix-span invariant. The first solver-facing wrapper replaces the bare current-pivot hypothesis in the latest explicit compact/norm-square QR certificate by the structured local determinant hypothesis. The stronger dual-budget wrapper also removes the separate dimensioned norm-square pivot-margin hypothesis by deriving it from the local leading determinant, local `kappaInf` budget, determinant-facing inverse-infinity bridge, and the displayed `m * budget_k^2 < 1 / K_k` dual compact-budget inequality. The direct inverse-budget companion removes the local `kappaInf` hypotheses from the latest route when the visible budget `(k+1)||nonsingInv(S_k)||_∞^2 <= K_k` is supplied instead. This is not a generic full-rank theorem; it requires nonsingular leading principal blocks in the stored no-pivot ordering and still keeps inverse/dual-budget assumptions visible. |
| `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_prefix_lower_zero_diagDominant_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_dualBudget` | theorem | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Diagonal-dominant companion for the latest structured explicit compact QR certificate. It composes `triInv_infNorm_sq_budget_of_diagDominantUpper_det_ne_zero` with the direct inverse-budget wrapper, so local diagonal dominance plus `det S_k != 0` and the displayed diagonal-minimum budget imply the direct `(k+1)||nonsingInv(S_k)||_∞^2 <= K_k` assumption. The dual compact-budget inequality remains visible. |

The factor theorem packages the same diagonal-lower field from a uniform contraction invariant `rowMax <= ρ |diag|` with `ρ <= 1`; the scalar-defect theorem packages the `ρ = 1` branch as the finite condition `max(rowMax - |diag|) <= 0`. The active-pivot witness also has positive scalar defect, so active max-pivoting plus active/off-diagonal budget control cannot supply this row-max condition either. This is a safe-direction bridge: it packages already-proved/offered source-control or row-max contraction data as `StoredQRDisplayedRowBudgetControl`; it does not derive off-diagonal domination or row-max defect from Cox--Higham row-growth estimates, active-pivot budget data, or ordinary no-pivot QR. |

| definition/theorems | `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Finite stage-diagonal lower-bound defect package for the active/prefix Cox--Higham route. The scalar budget is the finite maximum of `stageBudget k - |(S_k)_{ii}|` over displayed off-diagonal rows `i < k`; nonpositivity extracts the pointwise diagonal lower-bound family. The row-max adapters show that if `stageBudget k` is no larger than the displayed strict-upper row maximum on every off-diagonal row, then either nonpositive row-max defect or local diagonal dominance implies the stage-diagonal scalar condition. The row-max-alone counterexample has nonpositive row-max defect but positive stage-diagonal defect when the stage budget overshoots the displayed diagonal, so row-max defect by itself cannot discharge the stage-diagonal scalar condition. The active-pivot row-max visible-assumption wrappers use the row-max bridge internally before invoking the active-pivot stage-diagonal source-control and solver wrappers. The active-pivot route-elimination theorems show neither nonpositive row-max defect nor the `stageBudget <= rowMax` comparison is implied by active max-pivoting plus active/off-diagonal budget bounds alone. The exact no-pivot Householder counterexample has positive stage-diagonal defect for the constant stage budget `2`, and the universal route-elimination theorem proves that exact recurrence plus valid squared norms and nonzero denominators do not imply this scalar condition. The stored-panel actual-unit strengthening uses the same exact FP witness with `fp.u = 0` to show that stored recurrence, signed-alpha choice, nonzero source denominators, and `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` still do not imply the scalar stage-diagonal condition. The diagonal-dominant stored-surface strengthening uses the exact stored sequence from `[[3,2],[0,1]]`, which remains locally diagonally dominant and satisfies the signed-alpha/stored/source-denominator/actual-unit fields, but the constant stage budget `4` still makes the stage-diagonal scalar defect positive. The source-control and solver wrappers consume this scalar condition together with the stored recurrence, global compact-step, completed-column, global-product, determinant, and either norm-square or `κ∞`/dual-budget nonbreakdown fields. The scalar budget is the finite maximum of `stageBudget k - rowMax(k,i)` over displayed off-diagonal rows `i < k`; nonpositivity extracts the displayed comparison, and the converse theorem packages a pointwise comparison proof back into the scalar condition. The scalar row-max bridge derives `storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget <= 0` from `storedQRRowMaxDiagDefectBudget <= 0` plus this scalar comparison condition, with a diagonal-dominant specialization through the existing row-max defect bridge. The exact no-pivot Householder counterexample has positive comparison defect for constant stage budget `3`, and the universal route-elimination theorem proves that exact recurrence plus signed squared-norm identities, nonzero denominators, and nonnegative stage budgets do not imply `storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget <= 0`. The stored-panel actual-unit strengthening uses the same exact FP witness with `fp.u = 0`, the stored recurrence, signed-alpha choice, nonzero source denominators, `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`, and nonnegative stage budgets to rule out deriving the comparison scalar on the current stored surface. The diagonal-dominant stored-surface strengthening uses the exact stored sequence from `[[3,2],[0,1]]`: it remains locally diagonally dominant and satisfies the signed-alpha/stored/source-denominator/actual-unit fields, but the constant stage budget `3` still makes the comparison scalar positive because the displayed strict-upper row maximum is `2`; the active-pivot strengthening proves the same stored sequence also satisfies the finite active max-pivot policy, so adding that policy still does not force the scalar comparison; the compact-product strengthening adds a zero RHS stored recurrence and exact/zero-RHS global compact-product smallness, and the finite-max strengthening adds the canonical finite-max compact-product scalar itself, but the comparison scalar is still not forced. The signed-stage global-budget strengthening uses a nonconstant nonnegative stage budget `0, 3, 10, ...`; the exact global compact-update budgets vanish and `coxHighamActiveRowGrowthFactor 2 <= 3`, so the finite global compact-budget recurrence holds, but the comparison scalar remains positive. The row-max-granted signed-stage strengthening shows the same witness also has nonpositive finite row-max/diagonal defect, so row-max defect control plus diagonal dominance, active pivoting, signed-stage global recurrence, and finite-max compact-product smallness still cannot force the comparison scalar. The determinant-strengthened signed-stage route shows the witness also has nonsingular displayed leading blocks, with determinants `3` and `-3` at the displayed stages, so determinant/nonbreakdown data plus row-max defect control still does not force the comparison scalar. The active-pivot route-elimination wrappers lift the existing pointwise comparison counterexamples to the scalar condition itself, showing that active-pivot active/off-diagonal budget data, even with compact-product smallness added, cannot imply the comparison scalar. The row-max-granted witness has nonpositive row-max defect but positive comparison defect, so even supplying `storedQRRowMaxDiagDefectBudget <= 0` does not make the comparison scalar automatic, with or without product smallness. This closes only finite-family packaging and rules out comparison shortcuts; the comparison defect itself still has to be proved from a concrete pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-controlled loop or kept visible. |

| definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean`, `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `Algorithms/TriangularForwardBound.lean` | Finite global compact-step, completed-column, and global compact-product handoffs for the active/prefix Cox--Higham QR route. The finite compact-step maximum dominates every compact update component at stage `t`, so one recurrence for `B_{t+1}` supplies the off-diagonal, active-block, and prefix-row one-step budget fields. The global compact-step maximum is nonnegative, and the same recurrence makes nonnegative stage budgets monotone on the algorithmic horizon `b <= n`, avoiding a separate monotonicity proof on that stage range. The `qrStageHorizonBudget` adapters clamp a budget sequence after `n`, so the completed-column global-product source-control and solver wrappers with suffix `_of_horizonBudget` reuse older globally monotone theorem surfaces without assuming any behavior beyond the QR horizon. The completed-column theorem derives old-column preservation from the stored QR lower-zero invariant plus the zero-prefix Householder support lemma. The global product budget is a finite maximum over all pivots and replaces the per-pivot compact-product hypotheses by the scalar condition `storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget < 1`; the `_lt_one_...` adapters prove that this scalar condition follows from either all finite-product expressions, the older natural-indexed per-pivot product field, or the exact/zero-compact condition `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget = 0`. The zero-budget theorem does not prove floating-point smallness when the relative budget is positive. The `diagDominant_factor_norm_bounds` adapter reduces product smallness to one scalar inequality using global bounds on the diagonal-dominant inverse factors and pivot-column norms. The finite-max budgets `storedQRDiagDominantInvFactorBudget` and `storedQRPivotColumnNormBudget` provide canonical choices for those two global bounds, so the `diagDominant_finite_max_smallness` theorem leaves only the scalar inequality for the canonical maxima; the `diagDominant_finite_max_relativeBudget_le` theorem further lets that canonical inequality be checked with a nonnegative explicit relative-budget cap `cmax` when `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget <= cmax`. The QR finite-sum cap `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget_le_mul_of_step_le` and the LS wrapper `diagDominant_finite_max_stepBudget_le` specialize this positive-budget threshold with `cmax = n * cStep` under a uniform one-step compact-panel relative cap. The Householder/QR cap adapters reduce that one-step cap to vector-level column and RHS compact relative caps, and `diagDominant_finite_max_columnRhsBudget_le` checks the scalar condition with the explicit sequence cap `n * (n * cCol + cRhs)`. The norm-budget bridge `householderCompactRelativeBudget_le_of_normBudget_le_mul`, panel adapter `householderCompactPanelRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_of_normBudget_le_mul`, stored-step adapter `storedQRCompactStepRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_of_normBudget_le_mul`, and LS product theorem `diagDominant_finite_max_columnRhsNormBudget_le` start one level earlier from primitive norm-budget bounds `B_col <= cCol * ||A_k(:,j)||_2` and `B_rhs <= cRhs * ||b_k||_2`, then use the same cap chain. The componentwise bridge `householderCompactNormBudget_le_mul_of_componentBudget_le_mul_abs`, relative adapter `householderCompactRelativeBudget_le_of_componentBudget_le_mul_abs`, panel adapter `householderCompactPanelRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_of_componentBudget_le_mul_abs`, stored-step adapter `storedQRCompactStepRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_of_componentBudget_le_mul_abs`, and LS product theorem `diagDominant_finite_max_columnRhsComponentBudget_le` start another level earlier from entrywise compact-budget bounds `budget_i <= c * |input_i|`. They do not prove the entrywise component inequalities, local diagonal dominance, or scalar smallness inequality from the concrete recurrence. The packaged-invariant constructor `StoredQROffDiagonalControlInvariant.of_diagDominant_finiteMaxSmallness` now uses the same canonical scalar condition plus local diagonal dominance to derive the full route-1 invariant, including local determinants and per-pivot product fields. The `finiteMaxSmallness` source-control and solver handoffs compose that canonical scalar condition into the diagonal-dominant route, replacing the raw global-product hypothesis on that theorem surface. The `κ∞`/dual-budget variants reuse the local leading-block inverse-budget route to derive the dimensioned norm-square nonbreakdown margin, while the concrete-dual finite-max solver wrapper reuses the local diagonal-dominant inverse-budget theorem to avoid exposing auxiliary `κ`, `K`, and dual compact-budget fields. The determinant-free concrete-dual wrapper also reuses `det_ne_zero_of_diagDominantUpper`, so local leading-block nonsingularity is no longer a separate field on that branch. The equation (8) objective assembly that consumes these handoffs is listed in the next row. Remaining obligations are proving local diagonal dominance or off-diagonal control and the positive-budget scalar product-smallness inequality for the canonical explicit reflector coefficient maximum from a concrete pivoted/sorted loop, or keeping them visible as domain assumptions. |
| `householderAbsDotBudget_le_vecNorm2_mul`, `householderCompactUpdateCoeff`, `householderCompactNormBudgetCoeff`, `householderCompactUpdateCoeff_nonneg`, `householderCompactNormBudgetCoeff_nonneg`, `householderCompactComponentBudget_le_updateCoeff_mul_norm`, `householderCompactNormBudget_le_normBudgetCoeff_mul`, `householderCompactRelativeBudget_le_normBudgetCoeff`, `householderCompactPanelRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_normBudgetCoeff`, `storedQRCompactStepNormBudgetCoeff`, `storedQRCompactStepRelativeBudget_le_mul_add_normBudgetCoeff`, `storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget_lt_one_of_diagDominant_finite_max_normBudgetCoeff_le` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean`, `Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Explicit norm-coefficient bridge for the compact Householder arithmetic. The local Cauchy--Schwarz theorem bounds `S = sum_i |v_i||b_i|` by `||v||_2 ||b||_2`; the component theorem bounds each explicit compact budget by `updateCoeff(v,beta) * ||b||_2 * |v_i| + u * |b_i|`; the norm theorem gives `householderCompactNormBudget <= householderCompactNormBudgetCoeff(v,beta) * ||b||_2`. This is the valid replacement for trying to prove a universal entrywise-relative cap `budget_i <= c * |b_i|`, which can fail for zero or tiny entries because the Householder update is nonlocal. The stored QR wrapper applies the same reflector coefficient to every column and RHS in one stage, and the least-squares finite-max theorem says that a uniform bound `cHH` on those stage coefficients plus the scalar smallness condition with `n * ((n * cHH) + cHH)` implies `storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget < 1`. Remaining obligations are a concrete-loop bound on `cHH`, local diagonal dominance/off-diagonal control, determinant/conditioning/nonbreakdown fields, and scalar smallness. |

`Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` | Route-elimination facts for the diagonal-dominance QR branch. The concrete matrix `[[1,2],[0,1]]` is upper triangular, has nonzero determinant, admits a visible finite `κ∞` certificate by taking its own local `kappaInf`, and is not diagonally dominant, so neither triangular shape plus nonzero diagonal, triangular nonsingularity, nor a finite conditioning budget alone can justify `IsDiagDominantUpper`. It also gives an exact QR-shaped witness `A = I * R` with `I` orthogonal and `R` upper triangular with nonzero diagonal, so final exact QR shape alone cannot supply diagonal dominance. The source-style exact trailing Householder recurrence strengthens the obstruction: with signed squared-norm scalars and nonzero denominators, the two-step no-pivot sequence starting from `[[1,2],[0,1]]` reaches `[[-1,-2],[0,-1]]`, which is still not diagonally dominant and has positive row-max, stage-diagonal, and comparison scalar defects. The stored-panel actual-unit strengthening uses an exact floating-point model with `fp.u = 0`, follows the stored recurrence with the signed-alpha equation and nonzero source denominators, and still fails diagonal dominance. The same stored-panel witness also fails the displayed active-pivot policy at stage zero because the second column has larger active trailing norm than the displayed first pivot. The active-pivot budget witness separately rules out deriving either nonpositive row-max scalar defect or `stageBudget <= rowMax` from active-pivot and active/off-diagonal budget assumptions alone. The strengthened universal theorems rule out deriving diagonal dominance, active-pivot choice, the row-max scalar defect condition, the constant-budget stage-diagonal scalar condition, or the stage-budget/row-max comparison from those weaker surfaces, so the finite-max diagonal-dominant route still needs a genuine off-diagonal-control/pivoted-loop invariant or visible domain assumptions. |

For rectangular QR, use the trailing active-vector row below, because the full-vector theorem by itself does not impose the zero prefix needed to preserve finished rows. |
| `householderPrefixPart`, `householderTrailingPart`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq`, `householderTrailingActiveVector`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_nonneg`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_exists_ne`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_pivot_ne_zero`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_nonneg_budget_lt_sqrt`, `abs_alpha_eq_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `budget_lt_abs_alpha_of_lt_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `signedHouseholderAlpha`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_sq`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_mul_pivot_nonpos`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq_sq`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq_mul_pivot_nonpos`, `abs_entry_le_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_pivot_le`, `budget_lt_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_lt_abs_active_entry`, `exists_active_entry_budget_lt_abs_of_dim_mul_budget_sq_lt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `budget_lt_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_dim_mul_budget_sq_lt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `householderPrefixPart_add_trailingPart`, `householderPrefixPart_support`, `householderTrailingActiveVector_zero_prefix`, `matMulVec_householder_eq_self_of_zero_prefix_support`, `matMulVec_householder_trailingActiveVector_eq_prefix_alpha_zero`, `matMulVec_householder_trailingActiveVector_eq_zero_of_pivot_lt`, `exact_trailing_householder_sequence_lower_zero`, `rectangular_topBlock_shape_facts_of_lower_zero` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Exact trailing Householder shape and scalar lower-bound substrate for rectangular QR. The trailing vector has zero prefix and is constructed from the current pivot-column tail, so the exact reflector preserves entries above the pivot, maps the pivot to `alpha`, and zeros entries below the pivot. The scalar bridges prove that a nonzero active trailing entry gives positive trailing squared norm, that a nonnegative budget below `sqrt(||x_tail||_2^2)` already implies positive trailing norm, that `alpha^2 = ||x_tail||_2^2` converts square-root norm lower bounds into `|alpha|` lower bounds, that the standard signed-alpha convention simultaneously supplies `alpha^2 = ||x_tail||_2^2` and `alpha*x_p <= 0`, that any active-entry magnitude lower bound `budget < |x_i|` implies the square-root trailing-norm budget, and that the dimensioned norm-square margin `m * budget^2 < ||x_tail||_2^2` both supplies an active-entry witness and directly supplies the square-root budget. The sequence theorem proves that an exact recurrence of these trailing reflectors yields a final lower-trapezoidal zero pattern. The top-block theorem turns that pattern into the solver-facing `[R;0]` shape, top transformed RHS vector, and upper-triangular `R`; the nonzero diagonal/rank condition remains a visible assumption for triangular solves. |
| `fl_householderStoredPanelStep`, `fl_householderStoredRhsStep`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_prefix_lower_zero`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_lower_zero`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_topBlock_shape_facts`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_prefix_diag_nonzero_of_step_diag_nonzero` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Stored rounded rectangular Householder QR shape substrate. The panel step uses the compact rounded update for active/trailing entries, preserves completed columns, and explicitly writes zeros below the current pivot; the RHS step preserves rows above the active pivot. The prefix sequence theorem proves that after `k` steps, entries below the diagonal in the first `k` completed columns are exactly zero. The prefix diagonal theorem proves that already written nonzero pivots remain nonzero at every intermediate step. The final sequence theorem specializes the zero pattern to the full lower-trapezoidal shape, and the top-block theorem converts it to `[R;0]`, top transformed RHS, and upper-triangular `R` facts. This closes the storage-shape part of the rounded loop; use the diagonal-budget row below to discharge nonzero diagonal entries from a concrete per-pivot nonbreakdown inequality. |
| `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_exists_ne`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_pivot_ne_zero`, `householderTrailingNorm2Sq_pos_of_nonneg_budget_lt_sqrt`, `abs_alpha_eq_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `budget_lt_abs_alpha_of_lt_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `signedHouseholderAlpha`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq_sq`, `signedHouseholderAlpha_sqrt_trailingNorm2Sq_mul_pivot_nonpos`, `abs_entry_le_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_pivot_le`, `budget_lt_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_lt_abs_active_entry`, `exists_active_entry_budget_lt_abs_of_dim_mul_budget_sq_lt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `budget_lt_sqrt_householderTrailingNorm2Sq_of_dim_mul_budget_sq_lt_trailingNorm2Sq`, `householderTrailingActiveVector_inner_self_ne_zero_of_pivot_ne_alpha`, `householder_pivot_ne_alpha_of_trailingNorm2Sq_pos_mul_nonpos`, `householderTrailingActiveVector_inner_self_ne_zero_of_trailingNorm2Sq_pos_mul_nonpos`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanelStep_diag_nonzero_of_budget_lt_abs_alpha`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_step_diag_nonzero`, `fl_householderStoredPanel_sequence_prefix_diag_nonzero_of_step_diag_nonzero`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_budget_lt_abs_alpha`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_pivot_ne_alpha`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_trailingNorm_pos_mul_nonpos`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_signed_alpha_trailingNorm_pos_sqrt_budget`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_prefix_diag_nonzero_of_signed_alpha_trailingNorm_pos_sqrt_budget`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_span_nonbreakdown_active_entry_budget`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_sequence_diag_nonzero_of_span_nonbreakdown_trailingNorm2Sq_budget` | theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Stored trailing Householder nonbreakdown bridge. The scalar trailing-active-vector theorem proves `v^T v != 0` from the concrete pivot condition `x_p != alpha`; the sign-choice bridge proves that pivot condition from `alpha^2 = ||x_tail||_2^2`, positive trailing norm, and `alpha * x_p <= 0`. The concrete `signedHouseholderAlpha` wrapper derives the squared-norm and sign hypotheses from the standard alpha-selection rule. The positive-norm bridges reduce `0 < ||x_tail||_2^2` either to a concrete nonzero active trailing entry or to a nonnegative compact budget strictly below `sqrt(||x_tail||_2^2)`, while the active-entry bridge proves that `budget < |x_i|` for any active trailing row `i` implies the required square-root trailing-norm budget. The dimensioned norm-margin bridge proves that `m * budget^2 < ||x_tail||_2^2` supplies such an active entry and now directly supplies the square-root budget consumed by the stored QR theorem. The square-root bridge then rewrites that norm budget into the exact pivot magnitude `|alpha|`. The one-step theorem proves that the rounded stored pivot entry is nonzero when its componentwise compact-update budget is strictly smaller than `|alpha|`; the sequence theorem then uses completed-column preservation to carry each written pivot to the final top-block diagonal, while the prefix sequence theorem gives the same preservation fact at intermediate steps for earlier pivots. The prefix-span active-entry and norm-square wrappers compose those budget bridges with the stored diagonal theorem, so the square-root expression need not be stated at the final stored-loop level. This does not prove a conditioning-to-norm-margin bound, but it replaces the opaque final diagonal and denominator hypotheses with concrete per-pivot FP, signed-alpha, nonbreakdown, and dimensioned norm-margin conditions used by the LSQRSolve wrapper. |
| `qrPivotCounterexample2`, `qrPivotCounterexample2_first_pivot_zero`, `qrPivotCounterexample2_det_ne_zero`, `qrPivotCounterexample2_first_leadingBlock_det_zero`, `not_forall_det_ne_zero_implies_first_pivot_ne_zero`, `not_forall_det_ne_zero_implies_all_leading_blocks_det_ne_zero` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Route-elimination facts for the no-pivot rectangular QR bottleneck. The `2 x 2` column-swap matrix is nonsingular, but its first unpivoted pivot and its first `1 x 1` leading QR block determinant are zero. Thus whole-matrix nonsingularity/full rank alone cannot discharge the current-pivot or all-leading-principal-minor nonbreakdown hypotheses; a final theorem must use pivoting, explicit no-breakdown/leading-minor assumptions, or a stronger computed-loop invariant. |
| `householderCompactComponentBudget_nonneg`, `fl_householderStoredRhsStep_componentwise_error_bound`, `fl_householderStoredRhsStep_forward_error_bound`, `fl_householderStoredPanelStep_column_componentwise_error_bound`, `fl_householderStoredPanelStep_column_forward_error_bound`, `fl_householderStoredPanelStep_HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError_of_budget` | theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | Stored-step perturbation contract for the rounded rectangular Householder QR route. Completed columns, pivot zeros, and RHS prefix entries contribute zero error under explicit exact-preservation/zeroing hypotheses; active/trailing entries reuse the compact dot-scale-subtract budget. The final theorem feeds these forward-error bounds into `HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError` under visible budget-domination hypotheses. It is not yet the full QR loop theorem, because the concrete trailing-reflector loop still has to discharge the preservation/zeroing hypotheses at each step and handle nonzero pivots. |
| `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanelStep_HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError_of_budget` | theorem | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | One-step concrete trailing-reflector stored QR perturbation theorem. It derives completed-column preservation, stored pivot zeroing, and RHS-prefix preservation from the lower-zero invariant and the exact trailing Householder vector facts, then applies the stored-step perturbation contract. The remaining hypotheses are explicit compact budget domination, the pre-step lower-zero invariant, and the nonzero Householder denominator. This is the step-level concrete loop dependency before the multi-step stored sequence theorem. |
| `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_rect_orthogonal_columnwise_vector_sequence_geometric`, `fl_householderStoredTrailingPanel_higham_columnwise_factorization` | theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Multi-step stored trailing Householder QR perturbation and final Higham-style factorization assembly. The sequence theorem builds the exact trailing reflector from each current stored pivot column, proves the lower-zero invariant needed by the one-step theorem, assumes explicit Householder denominator and compact budget domination hypotheses, and applies the source-faithful columnwise common-`Q` accumulation theorem. It yields one orthogonal factor `Q`, columnwise data perturbation radii `((1+c)^n-1)||A(:,j)||_2`, and RHS radius `((1+c)^n-1)||b||_2`. The final assembly theorem combines that perturbation theorem with the stored top-block shape facts, producing the same `Q`, perturbations `DeltaA`/`Deltab`, final identities `A_hat n = Q^T(A+DeltaA)` and `b_hat n = Q^T(b+Deltab)`, a final stored `[R;0]` shape, top transformed RHS, and upper-triangular `R`. It deliberately does not assert that the diagonal of `R` is nonzero; use the solver wrappers below only when a separate nonbreakdown/conditioning hypothesis is available. Use `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget` to compose this with rounded back substitution, use the pivot-error variant when the per-pivot inequality `budget_k < |alpha_k|` is available, use the pivot-ne-alpha wrapper when the Householder denominator is supplied by the concrete condition `A_hat[k,k] != alpha_k`, use the active-entry wrapper when prefix-span nonbreakdown and a concrete active-entry budget are available, use the norm-square wrapper when prefix-span nonbreakdown and `m * budget_k^2 < ||A_k(k:m,k)||_2^2` are available, use the nonsingular-leading-block wrapper when determinant/rank facts and a square-root pivot budget are available, and use the triangular-leading-block wrapper when the determinant facts are generated from visible triangular/nonzero-diagonal local block assumptions. |
| `HouseholderAppError`, `HouseholderAppError.of_forward_error` | structure/theorem | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean` | Backward-error contract for applying a Householder reflector, plus the exact algebraic adapter from a normwise vector forward-error bound to that contract using the rank-one perturbation `e b^T / ||b||_2^2` when `b != 0` and `Delta P = 0` when `b = 0`. |
| `fl_householderApplyExplicit`, `fl_householderApplyExplicit_forward_error_bound`, `fl_householderApplyExplicit_HouseholderAppError`, `fl_householderApplyExplicitPanel_HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean` | Concrete explicit-matrix rounded Householder application route. It reuses `fl_matVec` to apply an already formed reflector matrix, proves `||fl(Pb)-Pb||_2 <= gamma_m || |P| ||_F ||b||_2`, converts that bound to `HouseholderAppError`, and instantiates the source-faithful columnwise panel contract. This is not the compact Householder dot/scale/subtract primitive theorem. |
| `fl_householderApplyCompact`, `householderCompactComponentBudget`, `matMulVec_householder_eq_compact`, `fl_householderApplyCompact_componentwise_error_bound`, `fl_householderApplyCompact_forward_error_bound`, `fl_householderApplyCompact_HouseholderAppError_of_budget`, `fl_householderApplyCompactPanel_HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError_of_budget`, `fl_householderApplyCompactPanel_rect_orthogonal_columnwise_vector_sequence_geometric` | definitions/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderApply.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Concrete compact rounded Householder dot/scale/subtract route. The exact identity `P b = b - beta v (v^T b)` is formalized, the rounded implementation computes a dot product, scale, componentwise multiply, and subtraction, and the forward error is bounded by an explicit deterministic data-dependent component budget. The adapter to `HouseholderAppError` has a visible budget-domination side condition `||budget||_2 <= c ||b||_2`; no concentration or unproved stability event is assumed. The sequence adapter plugs compact panel/RHS updates into the existing columnwise geometric accumulation theorem. Use `LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_compact_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_normBudget` when the final QR shape/top-RHS hypotheses are also available. The stored-step perturbation row above handles the storage convention that preserves completed columns and writes pivot zeros. |
| `frobNormSqRect_eq_sum_vecNorm2Sq_cols`, `frobNormRect_le_of_col_vecNorm2_le` | theorems | `Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean` | Rectangular Frobenius/column bridge. The first theorem rewrites `||A||_F^2` as the sum of squared Euclidean norms of the columns; the second converts columnwise Euclidean bounds `||A_:j||_2 <= eta ||B_:j||_2` into `||A||_F <= eta ||B||_F` for `eta >= 0`. This is used by the compact Householder sequence-to-QR-solver bridge. |
| `HouseholderPanelAppError`, `householderPanelAppError_rect_orthogonal_matrix_vector_sequence_geometric` | structure/theorem | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Strong common panel/update contract for a rectangular Householder step. One shared perturbation matrix `Delta P` explains both the rectangular matrix-panel update and right-hand-side update. This remains useful as a strong interface, but the source-faithful Householder QR route is columnwise rather than shared-`Delta P`. |
| `HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError`, `HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError.of_vector_applications`, `HouseholderColumnwisePanelAppError.of_forward_errors`, `orthogonal_vector_sequence_one_step_fixedQ`, `rect_orthogonal_columnwise_vector_sequence_geometric`, `householderColumnwisePanelAppError_rect_orthogonal_columnwise_vector_sequence_geometric` | structures/theorems | `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderSpec.lean`, `Algorithms/QR/HouseholderQR.lean` | Source-faithful columnwise Householder panel/update route. Each matrix-panel column and the right-hand side may have its own vector-level perturbation matrix, while the exact reflector sequence is common and therefore yields one theoretical accumulated orthogonal factor `Q`. The forward-error constructor composes the new vector adapter columnwise. This matches the standard columnwise Householder QR backward-error analysis and avoids treating a stronger shared-`Delta P` contract as the active low-level obligation. |
| `QRSolveBackwardError` | structure | `Algorithms/QR/QRSolve.lean` | Backward-error contract for QR-based solve. |
| `SolverSpec` | structure | `Algorithms/IterativeRefinement.lean` | Abstract componentwise backward-stable solver. |
| `ResidualError` | structure | `Algorithms/IterativeRefinement.lean` | Componentwise residual-computation error contract. |
